    // Wrong type (e.g., string instead of int)\n210\t\tErrTypeLength:   SeverityMedium,    // String/collection length issue\n211\t\n212\t\t// Semantic errors (format, range, CategoryValidation, // String length or collection size is invalid\n250\t\n251\t\t// Semantic errors - data meaning validation\n252\t\tErrTypeFormat: CategoryValidation, // Value format is =============================================================================\n292\t// FIELD-BASED ERROR GROUPING\n293\t// =============================================================================\n294\t\n295\t// ErrorFieldGroup represents a collection of validation errors grouped by field name.\n296\t// This type is useful for organizing errors that occur across multiple fields\n297\t// and for presenting field-specific error summaries to users.\n298\ttype ErrorFieldGroup map[string][]ValidationError\n299\t\n300\t// NewErrorFieldGroup creates a new ErrorFieldGroup from a slice of ValidationErrors.\n301\t// Errors are grouped by their FieldName field. Errors without a FieldName are\n302\t// grouped under an empty string key.\n303\tfunc NewErrorFieldGroup(errors []ValidationError) ErrorFieldGroup {\n304\t\tgroup := make(ErrorFieldGroup)\n305\t\tfor _, err := range errors {\n306\t\t\tfieldName := err.FieldName\n307\t\t\tgroup[fieldName] = append(group[fieldName], err)\n308\t\t}\n309\t\treturn group\n310\t}\n311\t\n312\t// Add adds an error to the appropriate field group.\n313\tfunc (efg ErrorFieldGroup) Add(err ValidationError) {\n314\t\tfieldName := err.FieldName\n315\t\tefg[fieldName] = append(efg[fieldName], err)\n316\t}\n317\t\n318\t// GetErrors returns all errors for a specific field.\n319\t// Returns nil if the field has no errors.\n320\tfunc (efg ErrorFieldGroup) GetErrors(fieldName string) []ValidationError {\n321\t\treturn efg[fieldName]\n322\t}\n323\t\n324\t// GetFieldNames returns a list of all field names that have errors.\n325\t// The list is sorted alphabetically for consistent ordering.\n326\tfunc (efg ErrorFieldGroup) GetFieldNames() []string {\n327\t\tnames := make([]string, 0, len(efg))\n328\t\tfor name := range efg {\n329\t\t\tnames = append(names, name)\n330\t\t}\n331\t\treturn names\n332\t}\n333\t\n334\t// HasErrors returns true if the specified field has any errors.\n335\tfunc (efg ErrorFieldGroup) HasErrors(fieldName string) bool {\n336\t\treturn len(efg[fieldName]) > validate\n\nimport (\n\t\"fmt\"\n\t\"strings\"\n)\n\n// =============================================================================\n// ERROR SEVERITY ENUM\n// =============================================================================\n\n// ErrorSeverity represents the severity level of a validation error.\n// Severity levels help prioritize error handling and user communication.\ntype ErrorSeverity string\n\nconst (\n\t// SeverityCritical indicates a critical error that prevents the system from functioning.\n\t// These errors typically indicate complete failure and require immediate attention.\n\t// Examples: Authentication failures, critical service unavailability.\n\tSeverityCritical ErrorSeverity = \"critical\"\n\n\t// SeverityHigh indicates a high-severity error that significantly impacts functionality.\n\t// These errors prevent core features from working but may have workarounds.\n\t// Examples: Missing required data, invalid authentication tokens.\n\tSeverityHigh ErrorSeverity = \"high\"\n\n\t// SeverityMedium indicates a medium-severity error that partially impacts functionality.\n\t// These errors may affect non-critical features or have mitigations.\n\t// Examples: Optional fields missing, non-critical validation failures.\n\tSeverityMedium ErrorSeverity = \"medium\"\n\n\t// SeverityLow indicates a low-severity error with minimal impact.\n\t// These errors typically represent minor issues or deviations.\n\t// Examples: Formatting issues, minor data inconsistencies.\n\tSeverityLow ErrorSeverity = \"low\"\n\n\t// SeverityInfo indicates an informational message that doesn't represent a failure.\n\t// These are typically warnings or notices for attention.\n\t// Examples: Deprecation notices, informational messages.\n\tSeverityInfo ErrorSeverity = \"info\"\n)\n\n// String returns the string representation of the ErrorSeverity.\nfunc (es ErrorSeverity) String() string {\n\treturn string(es)\n}\n\n// IsValid returns true if this is a valid ErrorSeverity constant.\nfunc (es ErrorSeverity) IsValid() bool {\n\tswitch es {\n\tcase SeverityCritical, SeverityHigh, SeverityMedium, SeverityLow, SeverityInfo:\n\t\treturn true\n\tdefault:\n\t\treturn false\n\t}\n}\n\n// Description returns a human-readable description of this severity level.\nfunc (es ErrorSeverity) Description() string {\n\tswitch es {\n\tcase SeverityCritical:\n\t\treturn \"Critical error that prevents system functionality\"\n\tcase SeverityHigh:\n\t\treturn \"High-severity error that significantly impacts functionality\"\n\tcase SeverityMedium:\n\t\treturn \"Medium-severity error that partially impacts functionality\"\n\tcase SeverityLow:\n\t\treturn \"Low-severity error with minimal impact\"\n\tcase SeverityInfo:\n\t\treturn \"Informational message that doesn't represent a failure\"\n\tdefault:\n\t\treturn \"Unknown severity level\"\n\t}\n}\n\n// IsCritical returns true if this is SeverityCritical.\nfunc (es ErrorSeverity) IsCritical() bool {\n\treturn es == SeverityCritical\n}\n\n// IsHigh returns true if this is SeverityHigh or higher.\nfunc (es ErrorSeverity) IsHigh() bool {\n\treturn es == SeverityHigh || es == SeverityCritical\n}\n\n// IsMediumOrHigher returns true if this is SeverityMedium or higher.\nfunc (es ErrorSeverity) IsMediumOrHigher() bool {\n\treturn es == SeverityMedium || es == SeverityHigh || es == SeverityCritical\n}\n\n// IsLowOrHigher returns true if this is SeverityLow or higher (everything except Info).\nfunc (es ErrorSeverity) IsLowOrHigher() bool {\n\treturn es == SeverityLow || es == SeverityMedium || es == SeverityHigh || es == SeverityCritical\n}\n\n// Compare compares this severity to another severity.\n// Returns:\n//   - positive if this severity is higher\n//   - negative if this severity is lower\n//   - 0 if they are equal\nfunc (es ErrorSeverity) Compare(other ErrorSeverity) int {\n\tseverityOrder := map[ErrorSeverity]int{\n\t\tSeverityInfo:     0,\n\t\tSeverityLow:      1,\n\t\tSeverityMedium:   2,\n\t\tSeverityHigh:     3,\n\t\tSeverityCritical: 4,\n\t}\n\tdiff := severityOrder[es] - severityOrder[other]\n\tif diff > 0 {\n\t\treturn 1\n\t} else if diff < 0 {\n\t\treturn -1\n\t}\n\treturn 0\n}\n\n// ErrorSeverityFromString creates an ErrorSeverity from a string.\n// Returns SeverityInfo if the string doesn't match any known severity.\nfunc ErrorSeverityFromString(s string) ErrorSeverity {\n\tswitch ErrorSeverity(strings.ToLower(s)) {\n\tcase SeverityCritical:\n\t\treturn SeverityCritical\n\tcase SeverityHigh:\n\t\treturn SeverityHigh\n\tcase SeverityMedium:\n\t\treturn SeverityMedium\n\tcase SeverityLow:\n\t\treturn SeverityLow\n\tcase SeverityInfo:\n\t\treturn SeverityInfo\n\tdefault:\n\t\treturn SeverityInfo\n\t}\n}\n\n// =============================================================================\n// DEFAULT SEVERITY MAPPINGS\n// =============================================================================\n\n// defaultSeverityForErrorType defines the default severity for each error type.\nvar defaultSeverityForErrorType = map[string]ErrorSeverity{\n\t// HTTP status codes\n\tErrorTypeStatusCode:          SeverityHigh,\n\tErrorTypeStatusCodeRange:     SeverityHigh,\n\tErrorTypeStatusCodeClass:     SeverityMedium,\n\n\t// Content validation\n\tErrorTypeContentType:         SeverityMedium,\n\tErrorTypeResponseStructure:   SeverityHigh,\n\tErrorTypeResponseBody:        SeverityHigh,\n\tErrorTypeResponseEncoding:    SeverityLow,\n\n\t// Error messages\n\tErrorTypeErrorMessage:        SeverityHigh,\n\tErrorTypeErrorMessagePattern: SeverityMedium,\n\tErrorTypeErrorCode:           SeverityMedium,\n\tErrorTypeErrorDetail:         SeverityLow,\n\n\t// Headers\n\tErrorTypeCORSHeaders:         SeverityMedium,\n\tErrorTypeAuthHeaders:         SeverityCritical,\n\tErrorTypeCustomHeaders:       SeverityLow,\n\n\t// Schema and data validation\n\tErrorTypeJSONSchema:          SeverityHigh,\n\tErrorTypeDataValidation:      SeverityHigh,\n\tErrorTypeFieldValidation:     SeverityMedium,\n\tErrorTypeTypeValidation:      SeverityHigh,\n\n\t// Performance\n\tErrorTypeTimeout:             SeverityHigh,\n\tErrorTypeRateLimit:           SeverityMedium,\n\tErrorTypeRetryExceeded:       SeverityHigh,\n\n\t// Custom\n\tErrorTypeCustom:              SeverityMedium,\n\tErrorTypeUnknown:             SeverityLow,\n}\n\n// GetDefaultSeverityForErrorType returns the default severity for a given error type.\n// Returns SeverityLow if the error type is not recognized.\n// This function handles both string-based error types (e.g., \"status_code\")\n// and ErrorType enum values (e.g., \"required\", \"format\").\nfunc GetDefaultSeverityForErrorType(errorType string) ErrorSeverity {\n\t// First check the string-based error type map\n\tif severity, ok := defaultSeverityForErrorType[errorType]; ok {\n\t\treturn severity\n\t}\n\n\t// Check if this is an ErrorType enum value\n\tet := ErrorTypeFromString(errorType)\n\tif et.IsValid() && et != ErrTypeUnknown {\n\t\treturn GetSeverityForErrorTypeEnum(et)\n\t}\n\n\treturn SeverityLow\n}\n\n// =============================================================================\n// ERRORTYPE ENUM SEVERITY MAPPINGS\n// =============================================================================\n\n// defaultSeverityForErrorTypeEnum defines the default severity for ErrorType enum values.\n// This mapping is for the ErrorType enum from error_type.go (e.g., ErrTypeRequired, ErrTypeFormat).\nvar defaultSeverityForErrorTypeEnum = map[ErrorType]ErrorSeverity{\n\t// Structural errors (missing fields, wrong types)\n\tErrTypeRequired: SeverityHigh,      // Required field is missing\n\tErrTypeType:     SeverityHigh,      // Wrong type (e.g., string instead of int)\n\tErrTypeLength:   SeverityMedium,    // String/collection length issue\n\n\t// Semantic errors (format, range, value)\n\tErrTypeFormat: SeverityMedium,      // Format doesn't match pattern (e.g., email)\n\tErrTypeRange:  SeverityMedium,      // Value outside numeric range\n\tErrTypeValue:  SeverityLow,         // Value invalid for domain-specific reasons\n\n\t// Constraint errors\n\tErrTypeDuplicate: SeverityHigh,     // Duplicate value (uniqueness constraint)\n\tErrTypeConflict:  SeverityMedium,   // Conflict with other values/constraints\n\n\t// Fallback\n\tErrTypeUnknown: SeverityLow,        // Unknown error type\n}\n\n// GetSeverityForErrorTypeEnum returns the default severity for a given ErrorType enum value.\n// Returns SeverityLow if the error type is not recognized.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\tseverity := GetSeverityForErrorTypeEnum(ErrTypeRequired)\n//\t// Returns: SeverityHigh\nfunc GetSeverityForErrorTypeEnum(errorType ErrorType) ErrorSeverity {\n\tif severity, ok := defaultSeverityForErrorTypeEnum[errorType]; ok {\n\t\treturn severity\n\t}\n\treturn SeverityLow\n}\n\n// =============================================================================\n// ERRORTYPE ENUM CATEGORY MAPPINGS\n// =============================================================================\n\n// categoryForErrorTypeEnum defines the category for ErrorType enum values.\n// This mapping is for the ErrorType enum from error_type.go (e.g., ErrTypeRequired, ErrTypeFormat).\nvar categoryForErrorTypeEnum = map[ErrorType]ErrorCategory{\n\t// Structural errors - data structure validation\n\tErrTypeRequired: CategoryValidation, // Required field is missing\n\tErrTypeType:     CategoryValidation, // Value type is incorrect\n\tErrTypeLength:   CategoryValidation, // String length or collection size is invalid\n\n\t// Semantic errors - data meaning validation\n\tErrTypeFormat: CategoryValidation, // Value format is invalid\n\tErrTypeRange:  CategoryValidation, // Value is outside acceptable range\n\tErrTypeValue:  CategoryValidation, // Value is invalid\n\n\t// Constraint errors\n\tErrTypeDuplicate: CategoryValidation, // Duplicate value detected\n\tErrTypeConflict:  CategoryValidation, // Conflict with existing values\n\n\t// Fallback\n\tErrTypeUnknown: CategoryCustom, // Unknown error type\n}\n\n// GetCategoryForErrorTypeEnum returns the category for a given ErrorType enum value.\n// Returns CategoryCustom if the error type is not recognized.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\tcategory := GetCategoryForErrorTypeEnum(ErrTypeRequired)\n//\t// Returns: CategoryValidation\nfunc GetCategoryForErrorTypeEnum(errorType ErrorType) ErrorCategory {\n\tif category, ok := categoryForErrorTypeEnum[errorType]; ok {\n\t\treturn category\n\t}\n\treturn CategoryCustom\n}\n\n// GetCategoryForErrorTypeEnumString returns the category for a given ErrorType enum string value.\n// This is a convenience function that converts a string to ErrorType and then looks up the category.\n// Returns CategoryCustom if the error type is not recognized.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\tcategory := GetCategoryForErrorTypeEnumString(\"required\")\n//\t// Returns: CategoryValidation\nfunc GetCategoryForErrorTypeEnumString(errorTypeStr string) ErrorCategory {\n\tet := ErrorTypeFromString(errorTypeStr)\n\treturn GetCategoryForErrorTypeEnum(et)\n}\n\n// =============================================================================\n// FIELD-BASED ERROR GROUPING\n// =============================================================================\n\n// ErrorFieldGroup represents a collection of validation errors grouped by field name.\n// This type is useful for organizing errors that occur across multiple fields\n// and for presenting field-specific error summaries to users.\ntype ErrorFieldGroup map[string][]ValidationError\n\n// NewErrorFieldGroup creates a new ErrorFieldGroup from a slice of ValidationErrors.\n// Errors are grouped by their FieldName field. Errors without a FieldName are\n// grouped under an empty string key.\nfunc NewErrorFieldGroup(errors []ValidationError) ErrorFieldGroup {\n\tgroup := make(ErrorFieldGroup)\n\tfor _, err := range errors {\n\t\tfieldName := err.FieldName\n\t\tgroup[fieldName] = append(group[fieldName], err)\n\t}\n\treturn group\n}\n\n// Add adds an error to the appropriate field group.\nfunc (efg ErrorFieldGroup) Add(err ValidationError) {\n\tfieldName := err.FieldName\n\tefg[fieldName] = append(efg[fieldName], err)\n}\n\n// GetErrors returns all errors for a specific field.\n// Returns nil if the field has no errors.\nfunc (efg ErrorFieldGroup) GetErrors(fieldName string) []ValidationError {\n\treturn efg[fieldName]\n}\n\n// GetFieldNames returns a list of all field names that have errors.\n// The list is sorted alphabetically for consistent ordering.\nfunc (efg ErrorFieldGroup) GetFieldNames() []string {\n\tnames := make([]string, 0, len(efg))\n\tfor name := range efg {\n\t\tnames = append(names, name)\n\t}\n\treturn names\n}\n\n// HasErrors returns true if the specified field has any errors.\nfunc (efg ErrorFieldGroup) HasErrors(fieldName string) bool {\n\treturn len(efg[fieldName]) > 0\n}\n\n// Count returns the total number of errors across all fields.\nfunc (efg ErrorFieldGroup) Count() int {\n\ttotal := 0\n\tfor _, errors := range efg {\n\t\ttotal += len(errors)\n\t}\n\treturn total\n}\n\n// CountByField returns the number of errors for a specific field.\nfunc (efg ErrorFieldGroup) CountByField(fieldName string) int {\n\treturn len(efg[fieldName])\n}\n\n// FilterBySeverity returns a new ErrorFieldGroup containing only errors\n// of the specified severity or higher.\nfunc (efg ErrorFieldGroup) FilterBySeverity(minSeverity ErrorSeverity) ErrorFieldGroup {\n\tresult := make(ErrorFieldGroup)\n\tfor fieldName, errors := range efg {\n\t\tfor _, err := range errors {\n\t\t\terrSeverity := GetDefaultSeverityForErrorType(err.ErrorType)\n\t\t\tif errSeverity.Compare(minSeverity) >= 0 {\n\t\t\t\tresult[fieldName] = append(result[fieldName], err)\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\treturn result\n}\n\n// FilterByCategory returns a new ErrorFieldGroup containing only errors\n// of the specified category.\nfunc (efg ErrorFieldGroup) FilterByCategory(category ErrorCategory) ErrorFieldGroup {\n\tresult := make(ErrorFieldGroup)\n\tfor fieldName, errors := range efg {\n\t\tfor _, err := range errors {\n\t\t\tif GetCategoryForErrorType(err.ErrorType) == category {\n\t\t\t\tresult[fieldName] = append(result[fieldName], err)\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\treturn result\n}\n\n// ToMap converts the ErrorFieldGroup to a map[string][]ValidationError.\n// This is useful for serialization and external communication.\nfunc (efg ErrorFieldGroup) ToMap() map[string][]ValidationError {\n\treturn map[string][]ValidationError(efg)\n}\n\n// Summary returns a human-readable summary of the field errors.\n// The summary includes field names and error counts.\nfunc (efg ErrorFieldGroup) Summary() string {\n\tvar parts []string\n\tfor fieldName, errors := range efg {\n\t\tif fieldName == \"\" {\n\t\t\tparts = append(parts, fmt.Sprintf(\"general: %d errors\", len(errors)))\n\t\t} else {\n\t\t\tparts = append(parts, fmt.Sprintf(\"%s: %d errors\", fieldName, len(errors)))\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\treturn strings.Join(parts, \", \")\n}\n\n// =============================================================================\n// ERROR CATEGORY GROUP\n// analyzing errors by their type and understanding\n// which categories of validation are failing.\ntype ErrorCategoryGroup map[ErrorCategory][]ValidationError\n\n// NewErrorCategoryGroup creates a new make(ErrorCategoryGroup)\n\tfor _, err := range errors {\n\t\tcategory := GetCategoryForErrorType(err.ErrorType)\n\t\tgroup[category] = append(group[category], err)\n\t}\n\treturn group\n}\n\n// Add append(ecg[category], err)\n}\n\n// GetErrors returns all errors for a specific category.\n// Returns nil if the category has no errors.\nfunc (ecg ErrorCategoryGroup) GetErrors(category {\n\tcategories := make([]ErrorCategory, 0, len(ecg))\n\tfor category := range ecg {\n\t\tcategories = append(categories, category)\n\t}\n\treturn categories\n}\n\n// HasErrors returns true if the specified category has any errors.\nfunc (ecg ErrorCategoryGroup) HasErrors(category ErrorCategory) bool {\n\treturn len(ecg[category]) > 0\n}\n\n// Count returns the total number of errors across all \")\n}\n\n// =============================================================================\n// MULTI-DIMENSIONAL ERROR INDEX\n// FormatFieldListWith(fields []string, conjunction string) string {\n132\t\tif len(fields) == 0 {\n133\t\t\treturn \"\"\n134\t\t}\n135\t\n136\t\tif len(fields) == 1 {\n137\t\t\treturn context.\n177\t// This formats field references with their error category for better context.\n178\t//\n179\t// Parameters:\n180\t//   - errorType: The ErrorType enum value\n181\t//   - fieldName: The field name\n182\t//   - parent: Optional parent path\n183\t//\n184\t// Returns a formatted field reference string with category context.\n185\t//\n186\t// Example usage:\n187\t//\n188\t//\tref := FormatFieldReferenceWithCategory(ErrTypeRequired, \"email\", \"request\")\n189\t//\t// Returns: \"[Data Validation] request.email\"\n190\t//\n191\t//\tref := FormatFieldReferenceWithCategory(ErrTypeFormat, \"email\", \"\")\n192\t//\t// Returns: \"[Data Validation] email\"\n193\tfunc FormatFieldReferenceWithCategory(errorType ErrorType, fieldName string, parent string) string {\n194\t\tfieldRef := FormatFieldRef(fieldName, parent)\n195\t\tcategory := GetCategoryForErrorTypeEnum(errorType)\n196\t\tcategoryLabel := FormatCategory(category)\n197\t\n198\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"[%s] %s\", categoryLabel, fieldRef)\n199\t}\n200\t\n201\t// =============================================================================\n202\t// ERROR MESSAGE FORMATTING\n203\t// =============================================================================\n204\t\n205\t// FormatErrorMessage creates a standardized error message from components.\n206\t// This ensures error messages are consistently formatted.\n207\t//\n208\t// Parameters:\n209\t//   - errorType: The type of error (use ErrorType enum or string)\n210\t//   - message: The core error message\n211\t//   - fieldName: Optional field name where the error occurred\n212\t//\n213\t// Returns a formatted error message string.\n214\t//\n215\t// Example usage:\n216\t//\n217\t//\tmsg := FormatErrorMessage(\"required\", \"Field is required\", \"email\")\n218\t//\t// Returns: \"[required] email: Field is required\"\n219\t//\n220\t//\tmsg := FormatErrorMessage(\"format\", \"Invalid email format\", \"\")\n221\t//\t// Returns: \"[format] Invalid email format\"\n222\tfunc FormatErrorMessage(errorType string, message string, fieldName string) string {\n223\t\terrorType = strings.TrimSpace(errorType)\n224\t\tmessage = strings.TrimSpace(message)\n225\t\tfieldName = strings.TrimSpace(fieldName)\n226\t\n227\t\tvar builder strings.Builder\n228\t\n229\t\t// Add error type in brackets\n230\t\tif errorType != \"\" {\n231\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"[%s] \", errorType))\n232\t\t}\n233\t\n234\t\t// Add field name if present\n235\t\tif fieldName != \"\" {\n236\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"%s: \", fieldName))\n237\t\t}\n238\t\n239\t\t// Add message\n240\t\tbuilder.WriteString(message)\n241\t\n242\t\treturn builder.String()\n243\t}\n244\t\n245\t// FormatErrorMessageWithType creates a standardized error message using ErrorType enum.\n246\t// This provides type-safe error type specification and consistent formatting.\n247\t//\n248\t// Parameters:\n249\t//   - errorType: The ErrorType enum value\n250\t//   - message: The core error message\n251\t//   - fieldName: Optional field name where the error occurred\n252\t//\n253\t// Returns a formatted error message string.\n254\t//\n255\t// Example usage:\n256\t//\n257\t//\tmsg := FormatErrorMessageWithType(ErrTypeRequired, \"Field is required\", \"email\")\n258\t//\t// Returns: \"[required] email: Field is required\"\n259\t//\n260\t//\tmsg := FormatErrorMessageWithType(ErrTypeFormat, \"Invalid email format\", \"\")\n261\t//\t// Returns: \"[format] Invalid email format\"\n262\tfunc FormatErrorMessageWithType(errorType ErrorType, message string, fieldName string) string {\n263\t\treturn FormatErrorMessage(errorType.String(), message, fieldName)\n264\t}\n265\t\n266\t// FormatErrorWithSeverity creates an error message with severity information.\n267\t// This integrates ErrorType enum with automatic severity lookup.\n268\t//\n269\t// Parameters:\n270\t//   - errorType: The ErrorType enum value\n271\t//   - message: The core error message\n272\t//   - fieldName: Optional field name where the error occurred\n273\t//\n274\t// Returns a formatted error message string with severity prefix.\n275\t//\n276\t// Example usage:\n277\t//\n278\t//\tmsg := FormatErrorWithSeverity(ErrTypeRequired, \"Field is required\", \"email\")\n279\t//\t// Returns: \"[⚠] High [required] email: Field is required\"\n280\t//\n281\t//\tmsg := FormatErrorWithSeverity(ErrTypeFormat, \"Invalid format\", \"email\")\n282\t//\t// Returns: \"[■] Medium [format] email: Invalid format\"\n283\tfunc FormatErrorWithSeverity(errorType ErrorType, message string, fieldName string) string {\n284\t\tseverity := GetSeverityForErrorTypeEnum(errorType)\n285\t\tseverityLabel := FormatSeverityWithIndicator(severity)\n286\t\terrorTypeLabel := errorType.String()\n287\t\n288\t\tvar builder strings.Builder\n289\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"%s [%s] \", severityLabel, errorTypeLabel))\n290\t\n291\t\tif fieldName != \"\" {\n292\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"%s: \", fieldName))\n293\t\t}\n294\t\n295\t\tbuilder.WriteString(message)\n296\t\n297\t\treturn builder.String()\n298\t}\n299\t\n300\t// FormatErrorWithValues creates an error message showing expected vs actual values.\n301\t// This is useful for validation errors where values don't match expectations.\n302\t//\n303\t// Parameters:\n304\t//   - fieldName: The field name\n305\t//   - expected: The expected value\n306\t//   - actual: The actual value\n307\t//   - customMessage: Optional custom message prefix\n308\t//\n309\t// Returns a formatted error message with value comparison.\n310\t//\n311\t// Example usage:\n312\t//\n313\t//\tmsg := FormatErrorWithValues(\"status_code\", 200, 404, \"\")\n314\t//\t// Returns: \"status_code: expected 200, got 404\"\n315\t//\n316\t//\tmsg := FormatErrorWithValues(\"count\", 5, 3, \"Not enough items\")\n317\t//\t// Returns: \"count: Not enough items (expected 5, got 3)\"\n318\tfunc FormatErrorWithValues(fieldName string, expected interface{}, actual interface{}, customMessage string) string {\n319\t\tfieldName = strings.TrimSpace(fieldName)\n320\t\tcustomMessage = strings.TrimSpace(customMessage)\n321\t\n322\t\tvar builder strings.Builder\n323\t\n324\t\tif fieldName != \"\" {\n325\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"%s: \", fieldName))\n326\t\t}\n327\t\n328\t\tif customMessage != \"\" {\n329\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"%s \", customMessage))\n330\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"(expected %v, got %v)\", expected, actual))\n331\t\t} else {\n332\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"expected %v, got %v\", expected, actual))\n333\t\t}\n334\t\n335\t\treturn builder.String()\n336\t}\n337\t\n338\t// FormatErrorWithRange creates an error message for range validation failures.\n339\t// This is useful for numeric range validations or length validations.\n340\t//\n341\t// Parameters:\n342\t//   - fieldName: The field name\n343\t//   - minValue: The minimum allowed value\n344\t//   - maxValue: The maximum allowed value\n345\t//   - actualValue: The actual value that failed validation\n346\t//\n347\t// Returns a formatted range error message.\n348\t//\n349\t// Example usage:\n350\t//\n351\t//\tmsg := FormatErrorWithRange(\"age\", 0, 120, 150)\n352\t//\t// Returns: \"age: value 150 is outside valid range [0, 120]\"\n353\tfunc FormatErrorWithRange(fieldName string, minValue interface{}, maxValue interface{}, actualValue interface{}) string {\n354\t\tfieldName = strings.TrimSpace(fieldName)\n355\t\n356\t\tif fieldName != \"\" {\n357\t\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"%s: value %v is outside valid range [%v, %v]\",\n358\t\t\t\tfieldName, actualValue, minValue, maxValue)\n359\t\t}\n360\t\n361\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"value %v is outside valid range [%v, %v]\",\n362\t\t\tactualValue, minValue, maxValue)\n363\t}\n364\t\n365\t// FormatErrorWithPattern creates an error message for pattern validation failures.\n366\t// This is useful for format validations like email, URL, regex patterns.\n367\t//\n368\t// Parameters:\n369\t//   - fieldName: The field name\n370\t//   - pattern: The pattern that failed (can be a description)\n371\t//   - actualValue: The actual value that failed validation\n372\t//\n373\t// Returns a formatted pattern error message.\n374\t//\n375\t// Example usage:\n376\t//\n377\t//\tmsg := FormatErrorWithPattern(\"email\", \"must contain @\", \"invalidemail\")\n378\t//\t// Returns: \"email: value 'invalidemail' does not match pattern (must contain @)\"\n379\tfunc FormatErrorWithPattern(fieldName string, pattern string, actualValue interface{}) string {\n380\t\tfieldName = strings.TrimSpace(fieldName)\n381\t\tpattern = strings.TrimSpace(pattern)\n382\t\n383\t\tif fieldName != \"\" {\n384\t\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"%s: value '%v' does not match pattern (%s)\",\n385\t\t\t\tfieldName, actualValue, pattern)\n386\t\t}\n387\t\n388\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"value '%v' does not match pattern (%s)\",\n389\t\t\tactualValue, pattern)\n390\t}\n391\t\n392\t// =============================================================================\n393\t// ERROR TYPE FORMATTING\n394\t// =============================================================================\n395\t\n396\t// FormatErrorType creates a user-friendly label from an ErrorType enum.\n397\t// This converts technical error type names to readable labels.\n398\t//\n399\t// Parameters:\n400\t//   - errorType: The ErrorType enum value\n401\t//\n402\t// Returns a formatted error type label.\n403\t//\n404\t// Example usage:\n405\t//\n406\t//\tlabel := FormatErrorType(ErrTypeRequired)\n407\t//\t// Returns: \"Required Field\"\n408\t//\n409\t//\tlabel := FormatErrorType(ErrTypeFormat)\n410\t//\t// Returns: \"Format Error\"\n411\tfunc FormatErrorType(errorType ErrorType) string {\n412\t\tswitch errorType {\n413\t\tcase ErrTypeRequired:\n414\t\t\treturn \"Required Field\"\n415\t\tcase ErrTypeFormat:\n416\t\t\treturn \"Format Error\"\n417\t\tcase ErrTypeRange:\n418\t\t\treturn \"Range Error\"\n419\t\tcase ErrTypeLength:\n420\t\t\treturn \"Length Error\"\n421\t\tcase ErrTypeType:\n422\t\t\treturn \"Type Error\"\n423\t\tcase ErrTypeValue:\n424\t\t\treturn \"Invalid Value\"\n425\t\tcase ErrTypeDuplicate:\n426\t\t\treturn \"Duplicate Value\"\n427\t\tcase ErrTypeConflict:\n428\t\t\treturn \"Conflict\"\n429\t\tcase ErrTypeUnknown:\n430\t\t\treturn \"Unknown Error\"\n431\t\tdefault:\n432\t\t\treturn string(errorType)\n433\t\t}\n434\t}\n435\t\n436\t// FormatErrorTypeFrom creates a user-friendly label from a string error type.\n437\t// This converts technical error type strings to readable labels.\n438\t//\n439\t// Parameters:\n440\t//   - errorTypeStr: The error type string\n441\t//\n442\t// Returns a formatted error type label.\n443\t//\n444\t// Example usage:\n445\t//\n446\t//\tlabel := FormatErrorTypeFrom(\"required\")\n447\t//\t// Returns: \"Required Field\"\n448\t//\n449\t//\tlabel := FormatErrorTypeFrom(\"format\")\n450\t//\t// Returns: \"Format Error\"\n451\tfunc FormatErrorTypeFrom(errorTypeStr string) string {\n452\t\tet := ErrorTypeFromString(errorTypeStr)\n453\t\treturn FormatErrorType(et)\n454\t}\n455\t\n456\t// =============================================================================\n457\t// CATEGORY FORMATTING\n458\t// =============================================================================\n459\t\n460\t// FormatCategory creates a user-friendly label from an ErrorCategory.\n461\t// This converts technical category names to readable labels.\n462\t//\n463\t// Parameters:\n464\t//   - category: The ErrorCategory value\n465\t//\n466\t// Returns a formatted category label.\n467\t//\n468\t// Example usage:\n469\t//\n470\t//\tlabel := FormatCategory(CategoryHTTP)\n471\t//\t// Returns: \"HTTP Protocol\"\n472\t//\n473\t//\tlabel := FormatCategory(CategoryValidation)\n474\t//\t// Returns: \"Data Validation\"\n475\tfunc FormatCategory(category ErrorCategory) string {\n476\t\tswitch category {\n477\t\tcase CategoryHTTP:\n478\t\t\treturn \"HTTP Protocol\"\n479\t\tcase CategoryContent:\n480\t\t\treturn \"Content\"\n481\t\tcase CategoryValidation:\n482\t\t\treturn \"Data Validation\"\n483\t\tcase CategoryPerformance:\n484\t\t\treturn \"Performance\"\n485\t\tcase CategorySecurity:\n486\t\t\treturn \"Security\"\n487\t\tcase CategoryCustom:\n488\t\t\treturn \"Custom\"\n489\t\tdefault:\n490\t\t\treturn string(category)\n491\t\t}\n492\t}\n493\t\n494\t// FormatErrorByCategory creates a category-aware error message.\n495\t// This formats errors according to their category, including appropriate\n496\t// contextual information based on the error type's category.\n497\t//\n498\t// Parameters:\n499\t//   - errorType: The ErrorType enum value\n500\t//   - message: The core error message\n501\t//   - fieldName: Optional field name where the error occurred\n502\t//\n503\t// Returns a formatted error message string with category context.\n504\t//\n505\t// Example usage:\n506\t//\n507\t//\tmsg := FormatErrorByCategory(ErrTypeRequired, \"Field is required\", \"email\")\n508\t//\t// Returns: \"[Data Validation] [required] email: Field is required\"\n509\t//\n510\t//\tmsg := FormatErrorByCategory(ErrTypeFormat, \"Invalid format\", \"email\")\n511\t//\t// Returns: \"[Data Validation] [format] email: Invalid format\"\n512\tfunc FormatErrorByCategory(errorType ErrorType, message string, fieldName string) string {\n513\t\tcategory := GetCategoryForErrorTypeEnum(errorType)\n514\t\tcategoryLabel := FormatCategory(category)\n515\t\n516\t\tvar builder strings.Builder\n517\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"[%s] \", categoryLabel))\n518\t\tbuilder.WriteString(FormatErrorMessageWithType(errorType, message, fieldName))\n519\t\n520\t\treturn builder.String()\n521\t}\n522\t\n523\t// FormatErrorWithCategoryAndSeverity creates a comprehensive error message\n524\t// with both category and severity information.\n525\t//\n526\t// Parameters:\n527\t//   - errorType: The ErrorType enum value\n528\t//   - message: The core error message\n529\t//   - fieldName: Optional field name where the error occurred\n530\t//\n531\t// Returns a formatted error message string with severity, category, and error type.\n532\t//\n533\t// Example usage:\n534\t//\n535\t//\tmsg := FormatErrorWithCategoryAndSeverity(ErrTypeRequired, \"Field is required\", \"email\")\n536\t//\t// Returns: \"[⚠] High [Data Validation] [required] email: Field is required\"\n537\tfunc FormatErrorWithCategoryAndSeverity(errorType ErrorType, message string, fieldName string) string {\n538\t\tseverity := GetSeverityForErrorTypeEnum(errorType)\n539\t\tseverityLabel := FormatSeverityWithIndicator(severity)\n540\t\tcategory := GetCategoryForErrorTypeEnum(errorType)\n541\t\tcategoryLabel := FormatCategory(category)\n542\t\n543\t\tvar builder strings.Builder\n544\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"%s [%s] \", severityLabel, categoryLabel))\n545\t\tbuilder.WriteString(FormatErrorMessageWithType(errorType, message, fieldName))\n546\t\n547\t\treturn builder.String()\n548\t}\n549\t\n550\t// =============================================================================\n551\t// SEVERITY FORMATTING\n552\t// =============================================================================\n553\t\n554\t// FormatSeverity creates a user-friendly label from an ErrorSeverity.\n555\t// This converts technical severity names to readable labels.\n556\t//\n557\t// Parameters:\n558\t//   - severity: The ErrorSeverity value\n559\t//\n560\t// Returns a formatted severity label.\n561\t//\n562\t// Example usage:\n563\t//\n564\t//\tlabel := FormatSeverity(SeverityCritical)\n565\t//\t// Returns: \"CRITICAL\"\n566\t//\n567\t//\tlabel := FormatSeverity(SeverityHigh)\n568\t//\t// Returns: \"High\"\n569\tfunc FormatSeverity(severity ErrorSeverity) string {\n570\t\tswitch severity {\n571\t\tcase SeverityCritical:\n572\t\t\treturn \"CRITICAL\"\n573\t\tcase SeverityHigh:\n574\t\t\treturn \"High\"\n575\t\tcase SeverityMedium:\n576\t\t\treturn \"Medium\"\n577\t\tcase SeverityLow:\n578\t\t\treturn \"Low\"\n579\t\tcase SeverityInfo:\n580\t\t\treturn \"Info\"\n581\t\tdefault:\n582\t\t\treturn string(severity)\n583\t\t}\n584\t}\n585\t\n586\t// FormatSeverityWithIndicator adds visual indicators to severity labels.\n587\t// This is useful for console output or UI display.\n588\t//\n589\t// Parameters:\n590\t//   - severity: The ErrorSeverity value\n591\t//\n592\t// Returns a severity label with visual indicator.\n593\t//\n594\t// Example usage:\n595\t//\n596\t//\tlabel := FormatSeverityWithIndicator(SeverityCritical)\n597\t//\t// Returns: \"[!] CRITICAL\"\n598\t//\n599\t//\tlabel := FormatSeverityWithIndicator(SeverityHigh)\n600\t//\t// Returns: \"[⚠] High\"\n601\tfunc FormatSeverityWithIndicator(severity ErrorSeverity) string {\n602\t\tindicator := severityIndicator(severity)\n603\t\tlabel := FormatSeverity(severity)\n604\t\n605\t\tif indicator != \"\" {\n606\t\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"[%s] %s\", indicator, label)\n607\t\t}\n608\t\n609\t\treturn label\n610\t}\n611\t\n612\t// severityIndicator returns a visual indicator for a severity level.\n613\tfunc severityIndicator(severity ErrorSeverity) string {\n614\t\tswitch severity {\n615\t\tcase SeverityCritical:\n616\t\t\treturn \"!\"\n617\t\tcase SeverityHigh:\n618\t\t\treturn \"⚠\"\n619\t\tcase SeverityMedium:\n620\t\t\treturn \"■\"\n621\t\tcase SeverityLow:\n622\t\t\treturn \"○\"\n623\t\tcase SeverityInfo:\n624\t\t\treturn \"i\"\n625\t\tdefault:\n626\t\t\treturn \"?\"\n627\t\t}\n628\t}\n629\t\n630\t// =============================================================================\n631\t// COMPREHENSIVE ERROR FORMATTING\n632\t// =============================================================================\n633\t\n634\t// FormatValidationErrorFull creates a comprehensive, formatted error message from a ValidationError.\n635\t// This combines all relevant error information into a single, readable message.\n636\t// Unlike FormatValidationError in format_helpers.go, this takes a ValidationError struct.\n637\t//\n638\t// Parameters:\n639\t//   - err: The ValidationError to format\n640\t//   - includeSeverity: Whether to include severity information\n641\t//\n642\t// Returns a formatted error message string.\n643\t//\n644\t// Example usage:\n645\t//\n646\t//\terr := ValidationError{\n647\t//\t    ErrorType: string(ErrTypeRequired),\n648\t//\t    Message: \"Field is required\",\n649\t//\t    FieldName: \"email\",\n650\t//\t}\n651\t//\n652\t//\tmsg := FormatValidationErrorFull(err, true)\n653\t//\t// Returns: \"[High] [required] email: Field is required\"\n654\tfunc FormatValidationErrorFull(err ValidationError, includeSeverity bool) string {\n655\t\tvar builder strings.Builder\n656\t\n657\t\t// Add severity if requested\n658\t\tif includeSeverity {\n659\t\t\t// Try to get severity from ErrorType enum first\n660\t\t\tet := ErrorTypeFromString(err.ErrorType)\n661\t\t\tvar severity ErrorSeverity\n662\t\t\tif et.IsValid() && et != ErrTypeUnknown {\n663\t\t\t\tseverity = GetSeverityForErrorTypeEnum(et)\n664\t\t\t} else {\n665\t\t\t\t// Fall back to string-based error type lookup\n666\t\t\t\tseverity = GetDefaultSeverityForErrorType(err.ErrorType)\n667\t\t\t}\n668\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"%s \", FormatSeverityWithIndicator(severity)))\n669\t\t}\n670\t\n671\t\t// Add error type and field\n672\t\tbuilder.WriteString(FormatErrorMessage(err.ErrorType, err.Message, err.FieldName))\n673\t\n674\t\t// Add expected/actual values if present\n675\t\tif err.Expected != nil || err.Actual != nil {\n676\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(\" (\")\n677\t\t\tif err.Expected != nil {\n678\t\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"expected: %v\", err.Expected))\n679\t\t\t}\n680\t\t\tif err.Expected != nil && err.Actual != nil {\n681\t\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(\", \")\n682\t\t\t}\n683\t\t\tif err.Actual != nil {\n684\t\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"actual: %v\", err.Actual))\n685\t\t\t}\n686\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(\")\")\n687\t\t}\n688\t\n689\t\t// Add location if present\n690\t\tif err.Location != \"\" {\n691\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\" [%s]\", err.Location))\n692\t\t}\n693\t\n694\t\treturn builder.String()\n695\t}\n696\t\n697\t// FormatValidationErrorBrief creates a brief, one-line error message.\n698\t// This is useful for summaries and compact displays.\n699\t//\n700\t// Parameters:\n701\t//   - err: The ValidationError to format\n702\t//\n703\t// Returns a brief error message string.\n704\t//\n705\t// Example usage:\n706\t//\n707\t//\terr := ValidationError{\n708\t//\t    ErrorType: string(ErrTypeRequired),\n709\t//\t    Message: \"Field is required\",\n710\t//\t    FieldName: \"email\",\n711\t//\t}\n712\t//\n713\t//\tmsg := FormatValidationErrorBrief(err)\n714\t//\t// Returns: \"email: required\"\n715\tfunc FormatValidationErrorBrief(err ValidationError) string {\n716\t\tif err.FieldName != \"\" {\n717\t\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"%s: %s\", err.FieldName, err.ErrorType)\n718\t\t}\n719\t\n720\t\treturn err.ErrorType\n721\t}\n722\t\n723\t// =============================================================================\n724\t// ERROR LIST FORMATTING\n725\t// =============================================================================\n726\t\n727\t// FormatErrorList formats multiple errors into a readable list.\n728\t// This is useful for displaying multiple validation failures.\n729\t//\n730\t// Parameters:\n731\t//   - errors: Slice of ValidationErrors to format\n732\t//   - includeSeverity: Whether to include severity information\n733\t//\n734\t// Returns a formatted error list string.\n735\t//\n736\t// Example usage:\n737\t//\n738\t//\terrors := []ValidationError{{\n739\t//\t    ErrorType: string(ErrTypeRequired),\n740\t//\t    Message: \"Field is required\",\n741\t//\t    FieldName: \"email\",\n742\t//\t}, {\n743\t//\t    ErrorType: string(ErrTypeFormat),\n744\t//\t    Message: \"Invalid format\",\n745\t//\t    FieldName: \"password\",\n746\t//\t}}\n747\t//\n748\t//\tmsg := FormatErrorList(errors, true)\n749\t//\t// Returns:\n750\t//\t// 1. [High] [required] email: Field is required\n751\t//\t// 2. [High] [format] password: Invalid format\n752\tfunc FormatErrorList(errors []ValidationError, includeSeverity bool) string {\n753\t\tif len(errors) == 0 {\n754\t\t\treturn \"No errors\"\n755\t\t}\n756\t\n757\t\tvar lines []string\n758\t\tfor i, err := range errors {\n759\t\t\tprefix := fmt.Sprintf(\"%d. \", i+1)\n760\t\t\tformatted := FormatValidationErrorFull(err, includeSeverity)\n761\t\t\tlines = append(lines, prefix+formatted)\n762\t\t}\n763\t\n764\t\treturn strings.Join(lines, \"\\n\")\n765\t}\n766\t\n767\t// FormatErrorListSummary creates a summary of multiple errors.\n768\t// This provides a high-level overview of validation failures.\n769\t//\n770\t// Parameters:\n771\t//   - errors: Slice of ValidationErrors to summarize\n772\t//\n773\t// Returns a summary string with error counts by type.\n774\t//\n775\t// Example usage:\n776\t//\n777\t//\terrors := []ValidationError{{\n778\t//\t    ErrorType: string(ErrTypeRequired),\n779\t//\t    FieldName: \"email\",\n780\t//\t}, {\n781\t//\t    ErrorType: string(ErrTypeRequired),\n782\t//\t    FieldName: \"password\",\n783\t//\t}}\n784\t//\n785\t//\tsummary := FormatErrorListSummary(errors)\n786\t//\t// Returns: \"2 errors: required (2)\"\n787\tfunc FormatErrorListSummary(errors []ValidationError) string {\n788\t\tif len(errors) == 0 {\n789\t\t\treturn \"No errors\"\n790\t\t}\n791\t\n792\t\t// Count errors by type\n793\t\tcounts := make(map[string]int)\n794\t\tfor _, err := range errors {\n795\t\t\tcounts[err.ErrorType]++\n796\t\t}\n797\t\n798\t\t// Build summary parts\n799\t\tvar parts []string\n800\t\tfor errorType, count := range counts {\n801\t\t\tparts = append(parts, fmt.Sprintf(\"%s (%d)\", errorType, count))\n802\t\t}\n803\t\n804\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"%d error(s): %s\", len(errors), strings.Join(parts, \", \"))\n805\t}\n806\t\n807\t//
