validate\n\nimport (\n\t\"fmt\"\n\t\"strings\"\n)\n\n/*\nError Formatting Helpers\n\nThis file provides helper functions for formatting validation errors consistently.\nThese helpers work with the ErrorType enum, ErrorCategory, and ErrorSeverity types\nto produce human-readable, well-formatted error messages.\n*/\n\n// =============================================================================\n// FIELD REFERENCE FORMATTING\n// =============================================================================\n\n// FormatFieldRef creates a standardized field reference string with optional parent.\n// This ensures field names are consistently formatted across error messages.\n// Supports nested field paths and array index formatting.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - fieldName: The name of the field (e.g., \"email\", \"user.address\", \"users.0.email\")\n//   - parent: Optional parent path (e.g., \"request\", \"response.body\")\n//\n// Returns a formatted field reference string with array indices in bracket notation.\n// Empty or invalid paths return \"(unknown field)\" for consistent error display.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\tref := FormatFieldRef(\"email\", \"request\")\n//\t// Returns: \"request.email\"\n//\n//\tref := FormatFieldRef(\"email\", \"\")\n//\t// Returns: \"email\"\n//\n//\tref := FormatFieldRef(\"users.0.email\", \"response\")\n//\t// Returns: \"response.users[0].email\"\n//\n//\tref := FormatFieldRef(\"\", \"\")\n//\t// Returns: \"(unknown field)\"\nfunc FormatFieldRef(fieldName string, parent string) string {\n\tfieldName = strings.TrimSpace(fieldName)\n\tparent = strings.TrimSpace(parent)\n\n\tif fieldName == \"\" {\n\t\tif parent != \"\" {\n\t\t\t// Normalize parent path even when field is empty\n\t\t\tnormalizedParent := FormatFieldPath(parent)\n\t\t\tif normalizedParent != \"(unknown field)\" {\n\t\t\t\treturn normalizedParent\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\treturn parent\n\t\t}\n\t\treturn \"(unknown field)\"\n\t}\n\n\t// Normalize field name to handle array indices (e.g., \"users.0.email\" → \"users[0].email\")\n\tnormalizedFieldName := FormatFieldPath(fieldName)\n\n\t// Check if normalization resulted in unknown field indicator\n\tif normalizedFieldName == \"(unknown field)\" {\n\t\tif parent != \"\" {\n\t\t\treturn parent\n\t\t}\n\t\treturn \"(unknown field)\"\n\t}\n\n\tif parent != \"\" {\n\t\t// Normalize parent as well in case it contains array indices\n\t\tnormalizedParent := FormatFieldPath(parent)\n\n\t\t// If parent normalizes to unknown field, just return the field name\n\t\tif normalizedParent == \"(unknown field)\" {\n\t\t\treturn normalizedFieldName\n\t\t}\n\n\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"%s.%s\", normalizedParent, normalizedFieldName)\n\t}\n\n\treturn normalizedFieldName\n}\n\n// FormatFieldLocationWith creates a detailed field location string with context.\n// This is useful for error messages that need to show exactly where an error occurred.\n// Unlike FormatFieldLocation in format_helpers.go, this supports parent paths.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - fieldName: The field name\n//   - location: Additional location context (e.g., \"line 5\", \"position 123\")\n//   - parent: Optional parent path\n//\n// Returns a formatted location string.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\tloc := FormatFieldLocationWith(\"email\", \"line 5\", \"request\")\n//\t// Returns: \"request.email at line 5\"\n//\n//\tloc := FormatFieldLocationWith(\"email\", \"\", \"request\")\n//\t// Returns: \"request.email\"\nfunc FormatFieldLocationWith(fieldName string, location string, parent string) string {\n\tref := FormatFieldRef(fieldName, parent)\n\tlocation = strings.TrimSpace(location)\n\n\tif location != \"\" {\n\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"%s at %s\", ref, location)\n\t}\n\n\treturn ref\n}\n\n// FormatFieldListWith formats a list of field names into a readable string with conjunction.\n// Unlike FormatFieldList in format_helpers.go, this supports custom conjunctions.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - fields: Slice of field names\n//   - conjunction: The conjunction to use (e.g., \"and\", \"or\")\n//\n// Returns a formatted list string.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\tlist := FormatFieldListWith([]string{\"email\", \"password\"}, \"and\")\n//\t// Returns: \"email and password\"\n//\n//\tlist := FormatFieldListWith([]string{\"email\", \"password\", \"name\"}, \"and\")\n//\t// Returns: \"email, password, and name\"\nfunc FormatFieldListWith(fields []string, conjunction string) string {\n\tif len(fields) == 0 {\n\t\treturn \"\"\n\t}\n\n\tif len(fields) == 1 {\n\t\treturn fields[0]\n\t}\n\n\tif len(fields) == 2 {\n\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"%s %s %s\", fields[0], conjunction, fields[1])\n\t}\n\n\t// Oxford comma style\n\tallButLast := fields[:len(fields)-1]\n\tlast := fields[len(fields)-1]\n\n\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"%s, %s %s\", strings.Join(allButLast, \", \"), conjunction, last)\n}\n\n// FormatFieldReferenceWithType creates a field reference with ErrorType context.\n// This provides type-specific field reference formatting that includes\n// contextual information based on the error type.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - errorType: The ErrorType enum value\n//   - fieldName: The field name\n//   - parent: Optional parent path\n//\n// Returns a formatted field reference string with error type context.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\tref := FormatFieldReferenceWithType(ErrTypeRequired, \"email\", \"request\")\n//\t// Returns: \"[Required Field] request.email\"\n//\n//\tref := FormatFieldReferenceWithType(ErrTypeFormat, \"email\", \"\")\n//\t// Returns: \"[Format Error] email\"\nfunc FormatFieldReferenceWithType(errorType ErrorType, fieldName string, parent string) string {\n\tfieldRef := FormatFieldRef(fieldName, parent)\n\terrorTypeLabel := FormatErrorType(errorType)\n\n\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"[%s] %s\", errorTypeLabel, fieldRef)\n}\n\n// FormatFieldReferenceWithCategory creates a field reference with category context.\n// This formats field references with their error category for better context.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - errorType: The ErrorType enum value\n//   - fieldName: The field name\n//   - parent: Optional parent path\n//\n// Returns a formatted field reference string with category context.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\tref := FormatFieldReferenceWithCategory(ErrTypeRequired, \"email\", \"request\")\n//\t// Returns: \"[Data Validation] request.email\"\n//\n//\tref := FormatFieldReferenceWithCategory(ErrTypeFormat, \"email\", \"\")\n//\t// Returns: \"[Data Validation] email\"\nfunc FormatFieldReferenceWithCategory(errorType ErrorType, fieldName string, parent string) string {\n\tfieldRef := FormatFieldRef(fieldName, parent)\n\tcategory := GetCategoryForErrorTypeEnum(errorType)\n\tcategoryLabel := FormatCategory(category)\n\n\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"[%s] %s\", categoryLabel, fieldRef)\n}\n\n// =============================================================================\n// ERROR MESSAGE FORMATTING\n// =============================================================================\n\n// FormatErrorMessage creates a standardized error message from components.\n// This ensures error messages are consistently formatted.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - errorType: The type of error (use ErrorType enum or string)\n//   - message: The core error message\n//   - fieldName: Optional field name where the error occurred\n//\n// Returns a formatted error message string.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\tmsg := FormatErrorMessage(\"required\", \"Field is required\", \"email\")\n//\t// Returns: \"[required] email: Field is required\"\n//\n//\tmsg := FormatErrorMessage(\"format\", \"Invalid email format\", \"\")\n//\t// Returns: \"[format] Invalid email format\"\nfunc FormatErrorMessage(errorType string, message string, fieldName string) string {\n\terrorType = strings.TrimSpace(errorType)\n\tmessage = strings.TrimSpace(message)\n\tfieldName = strings.TrimSpace(fieldName)\n\n\tvar builder strings.Builder\n\n\t// Add error type in brackets\n\tif errorType != \"\" {\n\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"[%s] \", errorType))\n\t}\n\n\t// Add field name if present\n\tif fieldName != \"\" {\n\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"%s: \", fieldName))\n\t}\n\n\t// Add message\n\tbuilder.WriteString(message)\n\n\treturn builder.String()\n}\n\n// FormatErrorMessageWithType creates a standardized error message using ErrorType enum.\n// This provides type-safe error type specification and consistent formatting.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - errorType: The ErrorType enum value\n//   - message: The core error message\n//   - fieldName: Optional field name where the error occurred\n//\n// Returns a formatted error message string.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\tmsg := FormatErrorMessageWithType(ErrTypeRequired, \"Field is required\", \"email\")\n//\t// Returns: \"[required] email: Field is required\"\n//\n//\tmsg := FormatErrorMessageWithType(ErrTypeFormat, \"Invalid email format\", \"\")\n//\t// Returns: \"[format] Invalid email format\"\nfunc FormatErrorMessageWithType(errorType ErrorType, message string, fieldName string) string {\n\treturn FormatErrorMessage(errorType.String(), message, fieldName)\n}\n\n// FormatErrorWithSeverity creates an error message with severity information.\n// This integrates ErrorType enum with automatic severity lookup.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - errorType: The ErrorType enum value\n//   - message: The core error message\n//   - fieldName: Optional field name where the error occurred\n//\n// Returns a formatted error message string with severity prefix.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\tmsg := FormatErrorWithSeverity(ErrTypeRequired, \"Field is required\", \"email\")\n//\t// Returns: \"[⚠] High [required] email: Field is required\"\n//\n//\tmsg := FormatErrorWithSeverity(ErrTypeFormat, \"Invalid format\", \"email\")\n//\t// Returns: \"[■] Medium [format] email: Invalid format\"\nfunc FormatErrorWithSeverity(errorType ErrorType, message string, fieldName string) string {\n\tseverity := GetSeverityForErrorTypeEnum(errorType)\n\tseverityLabel := FormatSeverityWithIndicator(severity)\n\terrorTypeLabel := errorType.String()\n\n\tvar builder strings.Builder\n\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"%s [%s] \", severityLabel, errorTypeLabel))\n\n\tif fieldName != \"\" {\n\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"%s: \", fieldName))\n\t}\n\n\tbuilder.WriteString(message)\n\n\treturn builder.String()\n}\n\n// FormatErrorWithValues creates an error message showing expected vs actual values.\n// This is useful for validation errors where values don't match expectations.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - fieldName: The field name\n//   - expected: The expected value\n//   - actual: The actual value\n//   - customMessage: Optional custom message prefix\n//\n// Returns a formatted error message with value comparison.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\tmsg := FormatErrorWithValues(\"status_code\", 200, 404, \"\")\n//\t// Returns: \"status_code: expected 200, got 404\"\n//\n//\tmsg := FormatErrorWithValues(\"count\", 5, 3, \"Not enough items\")\n//\t// Returns: \"count: Not enough items (expected 5, got 3)\"\nfunc FormatErrorWithValues(fieldName string, expected interface{}, actual interface{}, customMessage string) string {\n\tfieldName = strings.TrimSpace(fieldName)\n\tcustomMessage = strings.TrimSpace(customMessage)\n\n\tvar builder strings.Builder\n\n\tif fieldName != \"\" {\n\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"%s: \", fieldName))\n\t}\n\n\tif customMessage != \"\" {\n\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"%s \", customMessage))\n\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"(expected %v, got %v)\", expected, actual))\n\t} else {\n\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"expected %v, got %v\", expected, actual))\n\t}\n\n\treturn builder.String()\n}\n\n// FormatErrorWithRange creates an error message for range validation failures.\n// This is useful for numeric range validations or length validations.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - fieldName: The field name\n//   - minValue: The minimum allowed value\n//   - maxValue: The maximum allowed value\n//   - actualValue: The actual value that failed validation\n//\n// Returns a formatted range error message.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\tmsg := FormatErrorWithRange(\"age\", 0, 120, 150)\n//\t// Returns: \"age: value 150 is outside valid range [0, 120]\"\nfunc FormatErrorWithRange(fieldName string, minValue interface{}, maxValue interface{}, actualValue interface{}) string {\n\tfieldName = strings.TrimSpace(fieldName)\n\n\tif fieldName != \"\" {\n\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"%s: value %v is outside valid range [%v, %v]\",\n\t\t\tfieldName, actualValue, minValue, maxValue)\n\t}\n\n\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"value %v is outside valid range [%v, %v]\",\n\t\tactualValue, minValue, maxValue)\n}\n\n// FormatErrorWithPattern creates an error message for pattern validation failures.\n// This is useful for format validations like email, URL, regex patterns.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - fieldName: The field name\n//   - pattern: The pattern that failed (can be a description)\n//   - actualValue: The actual value that failed validation\n//\n// Returns a formatted pattern error message.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\tmsg := FormatErrorWithPattern(\"email\", \"must contain @\", \"invalidemail\")\n//\t// Returns: \"email: value 'invalidemail' does not match pattern (must contain @)\"\nfunc FormatErrorWithPattern(fieldName string, pattern string, actualValue interface{}) string {\n\tfieldName = strings.TrimSpace(fieldName)\n\tpattern = strings.TrimSpace(pattern)\n\n\tif fieldName != \"\" {\n\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"%s: value '%v' does not match pattern (%s)\",\n\t\t\tfieldName, actualValue, pattern)\n\t}\n\n\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"value '%v' does not match pattern (%s)\",\n\t\tactualValue, pattern)\n}\n\n// =============================================================================\n// ERROR TYPE FORMATTING\n// =============================================================================\n\n// FormatErrorType creates a user-friendly label from an ErrorType enum.\n// This converts technical error type names to readable labels.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - errorType: The ErrorType enum value\n//\n// Returns a formatted error type label.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\tlabel := FormatErrorType(ErrTypeRequired)\n//\t// Returns: \"Required Field\"\n//\n//\tlabel := FormatErrorType(ErrTypeFormat)\n//\t// Returns: \"Format Error\"\nfunc FormatErrorType(errorType ErrorType) string {\n\tswitch errorType {\n\tcase ErrTypeRequired:\n\t\treturn \"Required Field\"\n\tcase ErrTypeFormat:\n\t\treturn \"Format Error\"\n\tcase ErrTypeRange:\n\t\treturn \"Range Error\"\n\tcase ErrTypeLength:\n\t\treturn \"Length Error\"\n\tcase ErrTypeType:\n\t\treturn \"Type Error\"\n\tcase ErrTypeValue:\n\t\treturn \"Invalid Value\"\n\tcase ErrTypeDuplicate:\n\t\treturn \"Duplicate Value\"\n\tcase ErrTypeConflict:\n\t\treturn \"Conflict\"\n\tcase ErrTypeUnknown:\n\t\treturn \"Unknown Error\"\n\tdefault:\n\t\treturn string(errorType)\n\t}\n}\n\n// FormatErrorTypeFrom creates a user-friendly label from a string error type.\n// This converts technical error type strings to readable labels.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - errorTypeStr: The error type string\n//\n// Returns a formatted error type label.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\tlabel := FormatErrorTypeFrom(\"required\")\n//\t// Returns: \"Required Field\"\n//\n//\tlabel := FormatErrorTypeFrom(\"format\")\n//\t// Returns: \"Format Error\"\nfunc FormatErrorTypeFrom(errorTypeStr string) string {\n\tet := ErrorTypeFromString(errorTypeStr)\n\treturn FormatErrorType(et)\n}\n\n// =============================================================================\n// CATEGORY FORMATTING\n// =============================================================================\n\n// FormatCategory creates a user-friendly label from an ErrorCategory.\n// This converts technical category names to readable labels.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - category: The ErrorCategory value\n//\n// Returns a formatted category label.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\tlabel := FormatCategory(CategoryHTTP)\n//\t// Returns: \"HTTP Protocol\"\n//\n//\tlabel := FormatCategory(CategoryValidation)\n//\t// Returns: \"Data Validation\"\nfunc FormatCategory(category ErrorCategory) string {\n\tswitch category {\n\tcase CategoryHTTP:\n\t\treturn \"HTTP Protocol\"\n\tcase CategoryContent:\n\t\treturn \"Content\"\n\tcase CategoryValidation:\n\t\treturn \"Data Validation\"\n\tcase CategoryPerformance:\n\t\treturn \"Performance\"\n\tcase CategorySecurity:\n\t\treturn \"Security\"\n\tcase CategoryCustom:\n\t\treturn \"Custom\"\n\tdefault:\n\t\treturn string(category)\n\t}\n}\n\n// FormatErrorByCategory creates a category-aware error message.\n// This formats errors according to their category, including appropriate\n// contextual information based on the error type's category.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - errorType: The ErrorType enum value\n//   - message: The core error message\n//   - fieldName: Optional field name where the error occurred\n//\n// Returns a formatted error message string with category context.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\tmsg := FormatErrorByCategory(ErrTypeRequired, \"Field is required\", \"email\")\n//\t// Returns: \"[Data Validation] [required] email: Field is required\"\n//\n//\tmsg := FormatErrorByCategory(ErrTypeFormat, \"Invalid format\", \"email\")\n//\t// Returns: \"[Data Validation] [format] email: Invalid format\"\nfunc FormatErrorByCategory(errorType ErrorType, message string, fieldName string) string {\n\tcategory := GetCategoryForErrorTypeEnum(errorType)\n\tcategoryLabel := FormatCategory(category)\n\n\tvar builder strings.Builder\n\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"[%s] \", categoryLabel))\n\tbuilder.WriteString(FormatErrorMessageWithType(errorType, message, fieldName))\n\n\treturn builder.String()\n}\n\n// FormatErrorWithCategoryAndSeverity creates a comprehensive error message\n// with both category and severity information.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - errorType: The ErrorType enum value\n//   - message: The core error message\n//   - fieldName: Optional field name where the error occurred\n//\n// Returns a formatted error message string with severity, category, and error type.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\tmsg := FormatErrorWithCategoryAndSeverity(ErrTypeRequired, \"Field is required\", \"email\")\n//\t// Returns: \"[⚠] High [Data Validation] [required] email: Field is required\"\nfunc FormatErrorWithCategoryAndSeverity(errorType ErrorType, message string, fieldName string) string {\n\tseverity := GetSeverityForErrorTypeEnum(errorType)\n\tseverityLabel := FormatSeverityWithIndicator(severity)\n\tcategory := GetCategoryForErrorTypeEnum(errorType)\n\tcategoryLabel := FormatCategory(category)\n\n\tvar builder strings.Builder\n\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"%s [%s] \", severityLabel, categoryLabel))\n\tbuilder.WriteString(FormatErrorMessageWithType(errorType, message, fieldName))\n\n\treturn builder.String()\n}\n\n// =============================================================================\n// SEVERITY FORMATTING\n// =============================================================================\n\n// FormatSeverity creates a user-friendly label from an ErrorSeverity.\n// This converts technical severity names to readable labels.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - severity: The ErrorSeverity value\n//\n// Returns a formatted severity label.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\tlabel := FormatSeverity(SeverityCritical)\n//\t// Returns: \"CRITICAL\"\n//\n//\tlabel := FormatSeverity(SeverityHigh)\n//\t// Returns: \"High\"\nfunc FormatSeverity(severity ErrorSeverity) string {\n\tswitch severity {\n\tcase SeverityCritical:\n\t\treturn \"CRITICAL\"\n\tcase SeverityHigh:\n\t\treturn \"High\"\n\tcase SeverityMedium:\n\t\treturn \"Medium\"\n\tcase SeverityLow:\n\t\treturn \"Low\"\n\tcase SeverityInfo:\n\t\treturn \"Info\"\n\tdefault:\n\t\treturn string(severity)\n\t}\n}\n\n// FormatSeverityWithIndicator adds visual indicators to severity labels.\n// This is useful for console output or UI display.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - severity: The ErrorSeverity value\n//\n// Returns a severity label with visual indicator.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\tlabel := FormatSeverityWithIndicator(SeverityCritical)\n//\t// Returns: \"[!] CRITICAL\"\n//\n//\tlabel := FormatSeverityWithIndicator(SeverityHigh)\n//\t// Returns: \"[⚠] High\"\nfunc FormatSeverityWithIndicator(severity ErrorSeverity) string {\n\tindicator := severityIndicator(severity)\n\tlabel := FormatSeverity(severity)\n\n\tif indicator != \"\" {\n\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"[%s] %s\", indicator, label)\n\t}\n\n\treturn label\n}\n\n// severityIndicator returns a visual indicator for a severity level.\nfunc severityIndicator(severity ErrorSeverity) string {\n\tswitch severity {\n\tcase SeverityCritical:\n\t\treturn \"!\"\n\tcase SeverityHigh:\n\t\treturn \"⚠\"\n\tcase SeverityMedium:\n\t\treturn \"■\"\n\tcase SeverityLow:\n\t\treturn \"○\"\n\tcase SeverityInfo:\n\t\treturn \"i\"\n\tdefault:\n\t\treturn \"?\"\n\t}\n}\n\n// =============================================================================\n// COMPREHENSIVE ERROR FORMATTING\n// =============================================================================\n\n// FormatValidationErrorFull creates a comprehensive, formatted error message from a ValidationError.\n// This combines all relevant error information into a single, readable message.\n// Unlike FormatValidationError in format_helpers.go, this takes a ValidationError struct.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - err: The ValidationError to format\n//   - includeSeverity: Whether to include severity information\n//\n// Returns a formatted error message string.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\terr := ValidationError{\n//\t    ErrorType: string(ErrTypeRequired),\n//\t    Message: \"Field is required\",\n//\t    FieldName: \"email\",\n//\t}\n//\n//\tmsg := FormatValidationErrorFull(err, true)\n//\t// Returns: \"[High] [required] email: Field is required\"\nfunc FormatValidationErrorFull(err ValidationError, includeSeverity bool) string {\n\tvar builder strings.Builder\n\n\t// Add severity if requested\n\tif includeSeverity {\n\t\t// Try to get severity from ErrorType enum first\n\t\tet := ErrorTypeFromString(err.ErrorType)\n\t\tvar severity ErrorSeverity\n\t\tif et.IsValid() && et != ErrTypeUnknown {\n\t\t\tseverity = GetSeverityForErrorTypeEnum(et)\n\t\t} else {\n\t\t\t// Fall back to string-based error type lookup\n\t\t\tseverity = GetDefaultSeverityForErrorType(err.ErrorType)\n\t\t}\n\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"%s \", FormatSeverityWithIndicator(severity)))\n\t}\n\n\t// Add error type and field\n\tbuilder.WriteString(FormatErrorMessage(err.ErrorType, err.Message, err.FieldName))\n\n\t// Add expected/actual values if present\n\tif err.Expected != nil || err.Actual != nil {\n\t\tbuilder.WriteString(\" (\")\n\t\tif err.Expected != nil {\n\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"expected: %v\", err.Expected))\n\t\t}\n\t\tif err.Expected != nil && err.Actual != nil {\n\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(\", \")\n\t\t}\n\t\tif err.Actual != nil {\n\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"actual: %v\", err.Actual))\n\t\t}\n\t\tbuilder.WriteString(\")\")\n\t}\n\n\t// Add location if present\n\tif err.Location != \"\" {\n\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\" [%s]\", err.Location))\n\t}\n\n\treturn builder.String()\n}\n\n// FormatValidationErrorBrief creates a brief, one-line error message.\n// This is useful for summaries and compact displays.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - err: The ValidationError to format\n//\n// Returns a brief error message string.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\terr := ValidationError{\n//\t    ErrorType: string(ErrTypeRequired),\n//\t    Message: \"Field is required\",\n//\t    FieldName: \"email\",\n//\t}\n//\n//\tmsg := FormatValidationErrorBrief(err)\n//\t// Returns: \"email: required\"\nfunc FormatValidationErrorBrief(err ValidationError) string {\n\tif err.FieldName != \"\" {\n\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"%s: %s\", err.FieldName, err.ErrorType)\n\t}\n\n\treturn err.ErrorType\n}\n\n// =============================================================================\n// ERROR LIST FORMATTING\n// =============================================================================\n\n// FormatErrorList formats multiple errors into a readable list.\n// This is useful for displaying multiple validation failures.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - errors: Slice of ValidationErrors to format\n//   - includeSeverity: Whether to include severity information\n//\n// Returns a formatted error list string.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\terrors := []ValidationError{{\n//\t    ErrorType: string(ErrTypeRequired),\n//\t    Message: \"Field is required\",\n//\t    FieldName: \"email\",\n//\t}, {\n//\t    ErrorType: string(ErrTypeFormat),\n//\t    Message: \"Invalid format\",\n//\t    FieldName: \"password\",\n//\t}}\n//\n//\tmsg := FormatErrorList(errors, true)\n//\t// Returns:\n//\t// 1. [High] [required] email: Field is required\n//\t// 2. [High] [format] password: Invalid format\nfunc FormatErrorList(errors []ValidationError, includeSeverity bool) string {\n\tif len(errors) == 0 {\n\t\treturn \"No errors\"\n\t}\n\n\tvar lines []string\n\tfor i, err := range errors {\n\t\tprefix := fmt.Sprintf(\"%d. \", i+1)\n\t\tformatted := FormatValidationErrorFull(err, includeSeverity)\n\t\tlines = append(lines, prefix+formatted)\n\t}\n\n\treturn strings.Join(lines, \"\\n\")\n}\n\n// FormatErrorListSummary creates a summary of multiple errors.\n// This provides a high-level overview of validation failures.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - errors: Slice of ValidationErrors to summarize\n//\n// Returns a summary string with error counts by type.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\terrors := []ValidationError{{\n//\t    ErrorType: string(ErrTypeRequired),\n//\t    FieldName: \"email\",\n//\t}, {\n//\t    ErrorType: string(ErrTypeRequired),\n//\t    FieldName: \"password\",\n//\t}}\n//\n//\tsummary := FormatErrorListSummary(errors)\n//\t// Returns: \"2 errors: required (2)\"\nfunc FormatErrorListSummary(errors []ValidationError) string {\n\tif len(errors) == 0 {\n\t\treturn \"No errors\"\n\t}\n\n\t// Count errors by type\n\tcounts := make(map[string]int)\n\tfor _, err := range errors {\n\t\tcounts[err.ErrorType]++\n\t}\n\n\t// Build summary parts\n\tvar parts []string\n\tfor errorType, count := range counts {\n\t\tparts = append(parts, fmt.Sprintf(\"%s (%d)\", errorType, count))\n\t}\n\n\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"%d error(s): %s\", len(errors), strings.Join(parts, \", \"))\n}\n\n// =============================================================================\n// CONVENIENCE WRAPPER FUNCTIONS\n// =============================================================================\n\n// FormatFieldLocation is a convenience wrapper for FormatFieldLocationWith\n// without the parent parameter.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - fieldName: The field name\n//   - location: Additional location context\n//\n// Returns a formatted location string.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\tloc := FormatFieldLocation(\"email\", \"line 5\")\n//\t// Returns: \"email at line 5\"\nfunc FormatFieldLocation(fieldName string, location string) string {\n\treturn FormatFieldLocationWith(fieldName, location, \"\")\n}\n\n// FormatFieldList is a convenience wrapper for FormatFieldListWith\n// with \"and\" as the default conjunction.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - fields: Slice of field names\n//\n// Returns a formatted list string.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\tlist := FormatFieldList([]string{\"email\", \"password\"})\n//\t// Returns: \"email and password\"\nfunc FormatFieldList(fields []string) string {\n\treturn FormatFieldListWith(fields, \"and\")\n}\n\n// FormatValidationErrorToString is an alias for FormatValidationErrorFull.\n// This provides a more explicit name for converting a ValidationError to a string.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - err: The ValidationError to format\n//   - includeSeverity: Whether to include severity information\n//\n// Returns a formatted error message string.\nfunc FormatValidationErrorToString(err ValidationError, includeSeverity bool) string {\n\treturn FormatValidationErrorFull(err, includeSeverity)\n}\n\n// TruncateValue is an alias for TruncateString for compatibility.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - value: The value to truncate\n//   - maxLength: Maximum length (including ellipsis)\n//\n// Returns a truncated value string with ellipsis if needed.\nfunc TruncateValue(value string, maxLength int) string {\n\treturn TruncateString(value, maxLength)\n}\n\n// =============================================================================\n// HELPER FUNCTIONS\n// =============================================================================\n\n// QuoteValue wraps a value in quotes for display in error messages.\n// Empty values return \"(empty)\" instead of quotes.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - value: The value to quote\n//\n// Returns a quoted value string.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\tquoted := QuoteValue(\"email\")\n//\t// Returns: \"'email'\"\n//\n//\tquoted := QuoteValue(\"\")\n//\t// Returns: \"(empty)\"\nfunc QuoteValue(value string) string {\n\tif value == \"\" {\n\t\treturn \"(empty)\"\n\t}\n\n\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"'%s'\", value)\n}\n\n// TruncateString truncates a string value to a maximum length for display.\n// This is useful for keeping error messages concise.\n// Unlike TruncateValue in format_helpers.go, this takes a string and maxLength.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - value: The value to truncate\n//   - maxLength: Maximum length (including ellipsis)\n//\n// Returns a truncated value string with ellipsis if needed.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\ttruncated := TruncateString(\"very long string here\", 10)\n//\t// Returns: \"very long...\"\nfunc TruncateString(value string, maxLength int) string {\n\tif maxLength < 3 {\n\t\tmaxLength = 3\n\t}\n\n\tif len(value) <= maxLength {\n\t\treturn value\n\t}\n\n\treturn value[:maxLength-3] + \"...\"\n}\n\n// =============================================================================\n// ADDITIONAL ERRORTYPE ENUM INTEGRATION\n// =============================================================================\n\n// FormatErrorWithType formats an error message using the ErrorType enum.\n// This provides type-safe error formatting and automatic severity detection.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - errorType: The ErrorType enum value (e.g., ErrTypeRequired, ErrTypeFormat)\n//   - message: The error message content\n//   - fieldName: Optional field name where the error occurred\n//\n// Returns a formatted error message with automatic error type conversion.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\tmsg := FormatErrorWithType(ErrTypeRequired, \"Field is required\", \"email\")\n//\t// Returns: \"[required] email: Field is required\"\n//\n//\tmsg := FormatErrorWithType(ErrTypeFormat, \"Invalid email format\", \"\")\n//\t// Returns: \"[format] Invalid email format\"\nfunc FormatErrorWithType(errorType ErrorType, message string, fieldName string) string {\n\terrorTypeStr := errorType.String()\n\treturn FormatErrorMessage(errorTypeStr, message, fieldName)\n}\n\n// FormatErrorWithTypeAndSeverity formats an error message with explicit severity.\n// This combines ErrorType enum with custom severity levels for enhanced error display.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - errorType: The ErrorType enum value\n//   - severity: The ErrorSeverity level to use\n//   - message: The error message content\n//   - fieldName: Optional field name where the error occurred\n//\n// Returns a formatted error message with severity indicator.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\tmsg := FormatErrorWithTypeAndSeverity(ErrTypeRequired, SeverityHigh, \"Field is required\", \"email\")\n//\t// Returns: \"[⚠] High [required] email: Field is required\"\nfunc FormatErrorWithTypeAndSeverity(errorType ErrorType, severity ErrorSeverity, message string, fieldName string) string {\n\tvar builder strings.Builder\n\n\t// Add severity indicator\n\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"%s \", FormatSeverityWithIndicator(severity)))\n\n\t// Add error type, field, and message\n\tbuilder.WriteString(FormatErrorWithType(errorType, message, fieldName))\n\n\treturn builder.String()\n}\n\n// FormatErrorWithCategory formats an error message using ErrorCategory.\n// This provides category-aware error formatting for better error organization.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - category: The ErrorCategory value\n//   - message: The error message content\n//   - fieldName: Optional field name where the error occurred\n//\n// Returns a formatted error message with category label.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\tmsg := FormatErrorWithCategory(CategoryHTTP, \"Invalid status code\", \"response.status\")\n//\t// Returns: \"[HTTP Protocol] response.status: Invalid status code\"\nfunc FormatErrorWithCategory(category ErrorCategory, message string, fieldName string) string {\n\tcategoryLabel := FormatCategory(category)\n\treturn FormatErrorMessage(categoryLabel, message, fieldName)\n}\n\n// FormatErrorWithCategoryExplicit formats an error message with category and explicit severity.\n// This provides comprehensive error formatting with both category classification and severity.\n// Note: This differs from FormatErrorWithCategoryAndSeverity by taking explicit category and severity.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - category: The ErrorCategory value\n//   - severity: The ErrorSeverity level to use\n//   - message: The error message content\n//   - fieldName: Optional field name where the error occurred\n//\n// Returns a formatted error message with category and severity.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\tmsg := FormatErrorWithCategoryExplicit(CategoryValidation, SeverityHigh, \"Field is required\", \"email\")\n//\t// Returns: \"[⚠] High [Data Validation] email: Field is required\"\nfunc FormatErrorWithCategoryExplicit(category ErrorCategory, severity ErrorSeverity, message string, fieldName string) string {\n\tvar builder strings.Builder\n\n\t// Add severity indicator\n\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"%s \", FormatSeverityWithIndicator(severity)))\n\n\t// Add category and message\n\tbuilder.WriteString(FormatErrorWithCategory(category, message, fieldName))\n\n\treturn builder.String()\n}\n\n// =============================================================================\n// FIELD REFERENCE FORMATTING WITH EXPLICIT CATEGORY\n// =============================================================================\n\n// FormatFieldReferenceWithExplicitCategory formats a field reference with explicit ErrorCategory context.\n// This provides category-aware field reference formatting.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - fieldName: The field name\n//   - category: The ErrorCategory value\n//   - parent: Optional parent path\n//\n// Returns a formatted field reference with category context.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\tref := FormatFieldReferenceWithExplicitCategory(\"status_code\", CategoryHTTP, \"response\")\n//\t// Returns: \"response.status_code (HTTP Protocol)\"\nfunc FormatFieldReferenceWithExplicitCategory(fieldName string, category ErrorCategory, parent string) string {\n\tref := FormatFieldRef(fieldName, parent)\n\tcategoryLabel := FormatCategory(category)\n\n\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"%s (%s)\", ref, categoryLabel)\n}\n\n// =============================================================================\n// TYPE DETENTION AND AUTO-FORMATTING\n// =============================================================================\n\n// FormatErrorWithTypeDetection automatically detects ErrorType from string and formats.\n// This bridges the gap between string-based error types and the ErrorType enum.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - errorTypeStr: The error type string (e.g., \"required\", \"format\")\n//   - message: The error message content\n//   - fieldName: Optional field name where the error occurred\n//\n// Returns a formatted error message with detected type and automatic severity.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\tmsg := FormatErrorWithTypeDetection(\"required\", \"Field is required\", \"email\")\n//\t// Returns: \"[⚠] High [required] email: Field is required\"\n//\n//\tmsg := FormatErrorWithTypeDetection(\"format\", \"Invalid format\", \"email\")\n//\t// Returns: \"[■] Medium [format] email: Invalid format\"\nfunc FormatErrorWithTypeDetection(errorTypeStr string, message string, fieldName string) string {\n\terrorType := ErrorTypeFromString(errorTypeStr)\n\n\t// Use the detected type for category-aware formatting\n\treturn FormatErrorWithCategoryAndSeverity(errorType, message, fieldName)\n}\n\n// =============================================================================\n// ENHANCED VALIDATION ERROR FORMATTING\n// =============================================================================\n\n// FormatValidationErrorWithType creates a comprehensive error message using explicit ErrorType enum.\n// This is the enhanced version that allows overriding the error type.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - err: The ValidationError to format\n//   - errorType: The ErrorType enum value (overrides err.ErrorType)\n//   - includeSeverity: Whether to include severity information\n//\n// Returns a formatted error message with full error details.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\terr := ValidationError{\n//\t    ErrorType: string(ErrTypeRequired),\n//\t    Message: \"Field is required\",\n//\t    FieldName: \"email\",\n//\t}\n//\n//\tmsg := FormatValidationErrorWithType(err, ErrTypeRequired, true)\n//\t// Returns: \"[⚠] High [required] email: Field is required\"\nfunc FormatValidationErrorWithTypeOverride(err ValidationError, errorType ErrorType, includeSeverity bool) string {\n\tvar builder strings.Builder\n\n\t// Add severity if requested\n\tif includeSeverity {\n\t\t// Use the provided errorType for severity lookup\n\t\tseverity := GetSeverityForErrorTypeEnum(errorType)\n\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"%s \", FormatSeverityWithIndicator(severity)))\n\t}\n\n\t// Use the provided errorType instead of err.ErrorType\n\terrorTypeStr := errorType.String()\n\tbuilder.WriteString(FormatErrorMessage(errorTypeStr, err.Message, err.FieldName))\n\n\t// Add expected/actual values if present\n\tif err.Expected != nil || err.Actual != nil {\n\t\tbuilder.WriteString(\" (\")\n\t\tif err.Expected != nil {\n\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"expected: %v\", err.Expected))\n\t\t}\n\t\tif err.Expected != nil && err.Actual != nil {\n\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(\", \")\n\t\t}\n\t\tif err.Actual != nil {\n\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"actual: %v\", err.Actual))\n\t\t}\n\t\tbuilder.WriteString(\")\")\n\t}\n\n\t// Add location if present\n\tif err.Location != \"\" {\n\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\" [%s]\", err.Location))\n\t}\n\n\treturn builder.String()\n}\n\n// FormatValidationErrorWithAutoType creates an error message with automatic ErrorType detection.\n// This automatically converts the ValidationError's string error type to ErrorType enum.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - err: The ValidationError to format\n//   - includeSeverity: Whether to include severity information\n//\n// Returns a formatted error message with auto-detected type and severity.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\terr := ValidationError{\n//\t    ErrorType: \"required\",\n//\t    Message: \"Field is required\",\n//\t    FieldName: \"email\",\n//\t}\n//\n//\tmsg := FormatValidationErrorWithAutoType(err, true)\n//\t// Returns: \"[⚠] High [required] email: Field is required\"\nfunc FormatValidationErrorWithAutoType(err ValidationError, includeSeverity bool) string {\n\terrorType := ErrorTypeFromString(err.ErrorType)\n\treturn FormatValidationErrorWithTypeOverride(err, errorType, includeSeverity)\n}\n\n// =============================================================================\n// SEVERITY-AWARE ERROR LIST FORMATTING\n// =============================================================================\n\n// FormatErrorListBySeverity formats multiple errors grouped by severity level.\n// This provides a severity-organized view of validation failures.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - errors: Slice of ValidationErrors to format\n//   - minSeverity: Minimum severity level to include (use SeverityInfo for all)\n//\n// Returns a formatted error list grouped by severity.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\terrors := []ValidationError{{\n//\t    ErrorType: string(ErrTypeRequired),\n//\t    FieldName: \"email\",\n//\t}, {\n//\t    ErrorType: string(ErrTypeFormat),\n//\t    FieldName: \"password\",\n//\t}}\n//\n//\tmsg := FormatErrorListBySeverity(errors, SeverityMedium)\n//\t// Returns errors grouped by severity, starting from Medium\nfunc FormatErrorListBySeverity(errors []ValidationError, minSeverity ErrorSeverity) string {\n\tif len(errors) == 0 {\n\t\treturn \"No errors\"\n\t}\n\n\t// Filter by minimum severity manually\n\tvar filtered []ValidationError\n\tfor _, err := range errors {\n\t\t// Try to get severity from ErrorType enum first\n\t\terrorType := ErrorTypeFromString(err.ErrorType)\n\t\tvar errSeverity ErrorSeverity\n\t\tif errorType.IsValid() && errorType != ErrTypeUnknown {\n\t\t\terrSeverity = GetSeverityForErrorTypeEnum(errorType)\n\t\t} else {\n\t\t\t// Fall back to string-based error type lookup\n\t\t\terrSeverity = GetDefaultSeverityForErrorType(err.ErrorType)\n\t\t}\n\t\tif errSeverity.Compare(minSeverity) >= 0 {\n\t\t\tfiltered = append(filtered, err)\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\tif len(filtered) == 0 {\n\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"No errors at or above %s severity\", FormatSeverity(minSeverity))\n\t}\n\n\t// Group filtered errors by severity\n\tfilteredGroup := NewErrorSeverityGroup(filtered)\n\n\tvar lines []string\n\n\t// Process severities in order (critical to info)\n\tseverityOrder := []ErrorSeverity{SeverityCritical, SeverityHigh, SeverityMedium, SeverityLow, SeverityInfo}\n\n\tfor _, severity := range severityOrder {\n\t\tsevErrors := filteredGroup.GetErrors(severity)\n\t\tif len(sevErrors) == 0 {\n\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t}\n\n\t\t// Add severity header\n\t\tlines = append(lines, fmt.Sprintf(\"%s (%d errors):\", FormatSeverity(severity), len(sevErrors)))\n\n\t\t// Add errors under this severity\n\t\tfor _, err := range sevErrors {\n\t\t\tlines = append(lines, fmt.Sprintf(\"  - %s\", FormatValidationErrorWithAutoType(err, false)))\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\treturn strings.Join(lines, \"\\n\")\n}\n\n// FormatErrorListByCategory formats multiple errors grouped by category.\n// This provides a category-organized view of validation failures.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - errors: Slice of ValidationErrors to format\n//\n// Returns a formatted error list grouped by category.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\terrors := []ValidationError{{\n//\t    ErrorType: string(ErrTypeRequired),\n//\t    FieldName: \"email\",\n//\t}, {\n//\t    ErrorType: \"status_code\",\n//\t    FieldName: \"response.status\",\n//\t}}\n//\n//\tmsg := FormatErrorListByCategory(errors)\n//\t// Returns errors grouped by category (HTTP, Content, Validation, etc.)\nfunc FormatErrorListByCategory(errors []ValidationError) string {\n\tif len(errors) == 0 {\n\t\treturn \"No errors\"\n\t}\n\n\t// Create category group\n\tgroup := NewErrorCategoryGroup(errors)\n\n\tvar lines []string\n\n\t// Get all categories with errors\n\tcategories := group.GetCategories()\n\n\tfor _, category := range categories {\n\t\tcatErrors := group.GetErrors(category)\n\t\tif len(catErrors) == 0 {\n\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t}\n\n\t\t// Add category header\n\t\tcategoryLabel := FormatCategory(category)\n\t\tlines = append(lines, fmt.Sprintf(\"%s (%d errors):\", categoryLabel, len(catErrors)))\n\n\t\t// Add errors under this category\n\t\tfor _, err := range catErrors {\n\t\t\tlines = append(lines, fmt.Sprintf(\"  - %s\", FormatErrorMessage(err.ErrorType, err.Message, err.FieldName)))\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\treturn strings.Join(lines, \"\\n\")\n}\n","numLines":1286,"startLine":1,"totalLines":1286}}}

Unlike\n8\tValidationErrorType which is specific to HTTP/API validation, ErrorType provides\n9\tgeneric error type classifications for any validation scenario.\n10\t\n11\tCommon ErrorType values include:\n12\t  - ErrTypeRequired: Required field is missing or empty\n13\t  - ErrTypeFormat: Value format is invalid (e.g., email, UUID pattern)\n14\t  - ErrTypeRange: Value is outside acceptable range (min/max)\n15\t  - ErrTypeLength: String length or collection size is invalid\n16\t  - ErrTypeType: Value type is incorrect (e.g., string when int expected)\n17\t  - ErrTypeValue: Value is invalid for other reasons\n18\t  - ErrTypeDuplicate: Duplicate value detected\n19\t  - ErrTypeConflict: Conflict with existing values or constraints\n20\t  - ErrTypeUnknown: Unknown error type (default/fallback)\n21\t*/\n22\t\n23\timport (\n24\t\t\"fmt\"\n25\t\t\"strings\"\n26\t)\n27\t\n28\t// =============================================================================\n29\t// ERROR TYPE ENUM\n30\t// =============================================================================\n31\t\n32\t// ErrorType is a strongly-typed enum representing common validation error categories.\n33\t// It provides type safety and prevents typos when specifying error types.\n34\t//\n35\t// ErrorType represents fundamental validation failures that can occur in any\n36\t// validation context, unlike ValidationErrorType which is specific to HTTP/API validation.\n37\t//\n38\t// Use the ErrType* constants (e.g., ErrTypeRequired, ErrTypeFormat) for type-safe\n39\t// error type specification. values.\n52\tconst (\n53\t\t// ErrTypeRequired indicates a required field is missing or empty.\n54\t\t// Use when a value must be present but is not provided.\n55\t\t// Example: \"email field is required\"\n56\t\tErrTypeRequired ErrorType = \"required\"\n57\t\n58\t\t// ErrTypeFormat indicates a value format is invalid.\n59\t\t// Use when a value doesn't match the expected format pattern.\n60\t\t// Examples: email format, UUID pattern, date format\n61\t\t// Example: \"email format is invalid (must contain @)\"\n62\t\tErrTypeFormat ErrorType = \"format\"\n63\t\n64\t\t// ErrTypeRange indicates a value is outside acceptable numeric range.\n65\t\t// Use when a numeric value is less than minimum or greater than maximum.\n66\t\t// Example: \"age must be between 0 and 120\"\n67\t\tErrTypeRange ErrorType = \"range\"\n68\t\n69\t\t// ErrTypeLength indicates string length or collection size is invalid.\n70\t\t// Use when a value is too short or too long.\n71\t\t// Example: \"password must be at least 8 characters\"\n72\t\tErrTypeLength ErrorType = \"length\"\n73\t\n74\t\t// ErrTypeType indicates value type is incorrect.\n75\t\t// Use when a value is not of the expected type.\n76\t\t// Example: \"expected number, got string\"\n77\t\tErrTypeType ErrorType = \"type\"\n78\t\n79\t\t// ErrTypeValue indicates a value is invalid for domain-specific reasons.\n80\t\t// Use when a value is present and correctly formatted but still invalid.\n81\t\t// Example: \"country code 'XX' is not recognized\"\n82\t\tErrTypeValue ErrorType = \"value\"\n83\t\n84\t\t// ErrTypeDuplicate indicates a duplicate value was detected.\n85\t\t// Use when a value must be unique but already exists.\n86\t\t// Example: \"email 'user@example.com' already exists\"\n87\t\tErrTypeDuplicate ErrorType = \"duplicate\"\n88\t\n89\t\t// ErrTypeConflict indicates a conflict with existing values or constraints.\n90\t\t// Use when a value conflicts with business =============================================================================\n196\t\n197\t// ErrorTypeFromString creates an ErrorType from a string.\n198\t// Returns ErrTypeUnknown if the string doesn't match any known type.\n199\t//\n200\t// Example usage:\n201\t//\n202\t//\terrorType := ErrorTypeFromString(\"required\")\n203\t//\t// Returns: ErrTypeRequired\n204\t//\n205\t//\terrorType := ErrorTypeFromString(\"invalid_type\")\n206\t//\t// Returns: ErrTypeUnknown\n207\tfunc ErrorTypeFromString(s string) ErrorType {\n208\t\t// Check for exact match first (case-sensitive)\n209\t\tswitch validate\n\n/*\nErrorType enum - Common validation error type categories\n\nThis file defines the ErrorType enum, which represents fundamental validation\nerror categories that can be used across different validation contexts. Unlike\nValidationErrorType which is specific to HTTP/API validation, ErrorType provides\ngeneric error type classifications for any validation scenario.\n\nCommon ErrorType values include:\n  - ErrTypeRequired: Required field is missing or empty\n  - ErrTypeFormat: Value format is invalid (e.g., email, UUID pattern)\n  - ErrTypeRange: Value is outside acceptable range (min/max)\n  - ErrTypeLength: String length or collection size is invalid\n  - ErrTypeType: Value type is incorrect (e.g., string when int expected)\n  - ErrTypeValue: Value is invalid for other reasons\n  - ErrTypeDuplicate: Duplicate value detected\n  - ErrTypeConflict: Conflict with existing values or constraints\n  - ErrTypeUnknown: Unknown error type (default/fallback)\n*/\n\nimport (\n\t\"fmt\"\n\t\"strings\"\n)\n\n// =============================================================================\n// ERROR TYPE ENUM\n// =============================================================================\n\n// ErrorType is a strongly-typed enum representing common validation error categories.\n// It provides type safety and prevents typos when specifying error types.\n//\n// ErrorType represents fundamental validation failures that can occur in any\n// validation context, unlike ValidationErrorType which is specific to HTTP/API validation.\n//\n// Use the ErrType* constants (e.g., ErrTypeRequired, ErrTypeFormat) for type-safe\n// error type specification. When you need to use these as strings, use\n// string(ErrTypeRequired) or ErrTypeRequired.String().\n//\n// Usage example:\n//\n//\terr := ValidationError{\n//\t    ErrorType: string(ErrTypeRequired),\n//\t    Message:   \"Field 'email' is required\",\n//\t    FieldName: \"email\",\n//\t}\ntype ErrorType string\n\n// Common validation error type enum values.\nconst (\n\t// ErrTypeRequired indicates a required field is missing or empty.\n\t// Use when a value must be present but is not provided.\n\t// Example: \"email field is required\"\n\tErrTypeRequired ErrorType = \"required\"\n\n\t// ErrTypeFormat indicates a value format is invalid.\n\t// Use when a value doesn't match the expected format pattern.\n\t// Examples: email format, UUID pattern, date format\n\t// Example: \"email format is invalid (must contain @)\"\n\tErrTypeFormat ErrorType = \"format\"\n\n\t// ErrTypeRange indicates a value is outside acceptable numeric range.\n\t// Use when a numeric value is less than minimum or greater than maximum.\n\t// Example: \"age must be between 0 and 120\"\n\tErrTypeRange ErrorType = \"range\"\n\n\t// ErrTypeLength indicates string length or collection size is invalid.\n\t// Use when a value is too short or too long.\n\t// Example: \"password must be at least 8 characters\"\n\tErrTypeLength ErrorType = \"length\"\n\n\t// ErrTypeType indicates value type is incorrect.\n\t// Use when a value is not of the expected type.\n\t// Example: \"expected number, got string\"\n\tErrTypeType ErrorType = \"type\"\n\n\t// ErrTypeValue indicates a value is invalid for domain-specific reasons.\n\t// Use when a value is present and correctly formatted but still invalid.\n\t// Example: \"country code 'XX' is not recognized\"\n\tErrTypeValue ErrorType = \"value\"\n\n\t// ErrTypeDuplicate indicates a duplicate value was detected.\n\t// Use when a value must be unique but already exists.\n\t// Example: \"email 'user@example.com' already exists\"\n\tErrTypeDuplicate ErrorType = \"duplicate\"\n\n\t// ErrTypeConflict indicates a conflict with existing values or constraints.\n\t// Use when a value conflicts with business logic or other values.\n\t// Example: \"start date cannot be after end date\"\n\tErrTypeConflict ErrorType = \"conflict\"\n\n\t// ErrTypeUnknown indicates an unknown error type.\n\t// This is the default/fallback for unrecognized error types.\n\tErrTypeUnknown ErrorType = \"unknown\"\n)\n\n// =============================================================================\n// ERROR TYPE METHODS\n// =============================================================================\n\n// String returns the string representation of the ErrorType.\n// This implements the Stringer interface.\nfunc (et ErrorType) String() string {\n\treturn string(et)\n}\n\n// IsValid returns true if this is a valid ErrorType constant.\nfunc (et ErrorType) IsValid() bool {\n\tswitch et {\n\tcase ErrTypeRequired, ErrTypeFormat, ErrTypeRange, ErrTypeLength,\n\t\tErrTypeType, ErrTypeValue, ErrTypeDuplicate, ErrTypeConflict,\n\t\tErrTypeUnknown:\n\t\treturn true\n\tdefault:\n\t\treturn false\n\t}\n}\n\n// Description returns a human-readable description of this error type.\n// Returns \"Unknown error type\" if the type is not recognized.\nfunc (et ErrorType) Description() string {\n\tswitch et {\n\tcase ErrTypeRequired:\n\t\treturn \"Required field is missing or empty\"\n\tcase ErrTypeFormat:\n\t\treturn \"Value format is invalid\"\n\tcase ErrTypeRange:\n\t\treturn \"Value is outside acceptable range\"\n\tcase ErrTypeLength:\n\t\treturn \"String length or collection size is invalid\"\n\tcase ErrTypeType:\n\t\treturn \"Value type is incorrect\"\n\tcase ErrTypeValue:\n\t\treturn \"Value is invalid\"\n\tcase ErrTypeDuplicate:\n\t\treturn \"Duplicate value detected\"\n\tcase ErrTypeConflict:\n\t\treturn \"Conflict with existing values or constraints\"\n\tcase ErrTypeUnknown:\n\t\treturn \"Unknown error type\"\n\tdefault:\n\t\treturn \"Unknown error type\"\n\t}\n}\n\n// IsRequired returns true if this is ErrTypeRequired.\nfunc (et ErrorType) IsRequired() bool {\n\treturn et == ErrTypeRequired\n}\n\n// IsFormat returns true if this is ErrTypeFormat.\nfunc (et ErrorType) IsFormat() bool {\n\treturn et == ErrTypeFormat\n}\n\n// IsRange returns true if this is ErrTypeRange.\nfunc (et ErrorType) IsRange() bool {\n\treturn et == ErrTypeRange\n}\n\n// IsLength returns true if this is ErrTypeLength.\nfunc (et ErrorType) IsLength() bool {\n\treturn et == Convenience functions: Use pre-built functions like FormatStatusCodeError for common cases\n45\t  4. Custom validation: Use FormatCustomValidationError with FormatOption for advanced scenarios\n46\t\n47\tExample usage:\n48\t\n49\t\t// Using the ValidationFormatter builder\n50\t\terr := NewValidationFormatter(\"status_code\").\n51\t\t\tWithExpected(200).\n52\t\t\tWithActual(404).\n53\t\t\tWithContext(\"GET /api/users/123\").\n54\t\t\tFormat()\n55\t\t// Output:\n56\t\t// status_code validation failed\n57\t\t//   Expected: 200 (OK)\n58\t\t//   Actual:   404 (Not Found)\n59\t\t//   Context:  GET /api/users/123\n60\t\t//   Suggestions:\n61\t\t//     - Verify the endpoint URL is correct\n62\t\t//     - Check if the resource ID or identifier exists\n63\t\n64\t\t// Using a convenience function\n65\t\terr := FormatStatusCodeError(200, 404, \"GET /api/users/123\")\n66\t\n67\t# Error Interface\n68\t\n69\tValidationError implements the error interface, producing detailed, multi-line error messages\n70\tthat include all relevant validation information. The Error() method formats the output\n71\twith expected/actual values, context, field information, and suggestions for resolution.\n72\t\n73\t# Serialization\n74\t\n75\tValidationError includes JSON struct tags for all fields, enabling easy serialization:\n76\t\n77\t\terr := ValidationError{ErrorType: \"status_code\", Message: \"test\"}\n78\t\tjsonBytes, _ := json.Marshal(err)\n79\t\t// Output: {\"error_type\":\"status_code\",\"message\":\"test\"}\n80\t\n81\tThe ToMap() method provides flexible map[string]interface{} conversion for dynamic\n82\tmanipulation before serialization.\n83\t\n84\t# Validation\n85\t\n86\tThe Validate() method checks that a ValidationError is properly structured with\n87\trequired fields populated. It follows these design\nprinciples:\n\n  - Serializable: Full JSON marshaling/unmarshaling support via struct tags\n  - Machine-readable: Clear field names suitable for automated processing and log aggregation\n  - Human-readable: Descriptive messages with helpful suggestions for debugging\n  - Extensible: Optional fields provide additional context without breaking existing code\n\n# Required vs Optional Fields\n\nRequired fields that must be set for every ValidationError:\n  - ErrorType: The validation category (e.g., \"status_code\", \"error_message\", \"content_type\")\n  - Message: A concise, human-readable description of the validation failure\n\nOptional fields that provide additional context:\n  - Expected/Actual: The values that were expected and actually received\n  - Context: Where/when the validation occurred (e.g., endpoint, operation type)\n  - FieldName: Specific field where the error was found (e.g., in response body JSON)\n  - Location: Position information (e.g., \"line 5\", \"field 'user.email'\")\n  - RelatedFields: Other fields related to this error for additional context\n  - PatternDetails: Information about regex pattern matching failures\n  - RangeInfo: Range boundaries for range-based validations (e.g., \"4xx\", \"5xx\")\n  - ValidationDetails: Additional validation-specific information as a list\n  - ResponseSnippet: Truncated excerpt from the response for debugging\n  - Suggestions: Actionable recommendations for resolving the error\n\n# Creating Validation Errors\n\nThis package provides several ways to create ValidationError instances:\n\n  1. Direct struct initialization: Create ValidationError directly for full control\n  2. ValidationFormatter builder: Use the builder pattern for ergonomic, fluent construction\n  3. Convenience functions: Use pre-built functions like FormatStatusCodeError for common cases\n  4. Custom validation: Use FormatCustomValidationError with FormatOption for advanced scenarios\n\nExample usage:\n\n\t// Using the ValidationFormatter builder\n\terr := NewValidationFormatter(\"status_code\").\n\t\tWithExpected(200).\n\t\tWithActual(404).\n\t\tWithContext(\"GET /api/users/123\").\n\t\tFormat()\n\t// Output:\n\t// status_code validation failed\n\t//   Expected: 200 (OK)\n\t//   Actual:   404 (Not Found)\n\t//   Context:  GET /api/users/123\n\t//   Suggestions:\n\t//     - Verify the endpoint URL is correct\n\t//     - Check if the resource ID or identifier exists\n\n\t// Using a convenience function\n\terr := FormatStatusCodeError(200, 404, \"GET /api/users/123\")\n\n# Error Interface\n\nValidationError implements the error interface, producing detailed, multi-line error messages\nthat include all relevant validation information. The Error() method formats the output\nwith expected/actual values, context, field information, and suggestions for resolution.\n\n# Serialization\n\nValidationError includes JSON struct tags for all fields, enabling easy serialization:\n\n\terr := ValidationError{ErrorType: \"status_code\", Message: \"test\"}\n\tjsonBytes, _ := json.Marshal(err)\n\t// Output: {\"error_type\":\"status_code\",\"message\":\"test\"}\n\nThe ToMap() method provides flexible map[string]interface{} conversion for dynamic\nmanipulation before serialization.\n\n# Validation\n\nThe Validate() method checks that a ValidationError is properly structured with\nrequired fields populated. Use this to catch programming errors early:\n\n\terr := ValidationError{ErrorType: \"status_code\", Message: \"test\"}\n\tif err := err.Validate(); err != nil {\n\t\tlog.Printf(\"Invalid error structure: %v\", err)\n\t}\n*/\n\nimport (\n\t\"fmt\"\n\t\"strings\"\n)\n\n// =============================================================================","numLines":100,"startLine":1,"totalLines":663}}}

{"type":"assistant","message":{"id":"msg_202607142218569dbaf5eab1404c5e","type":"message","role":"assistant","model":"glm-4.7","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"call_69791addc25647d8942deb0e","name":"Write","input":{"file_path":"/home/coding/ARMOR/notes/bf-mtv96n.md","content":"# ErrorType and Category Types Structure Analysis\n\n## Overview\n\nThe ARMOR validation package has **two parallel ErrorType systems** that serve different purposes:\n\n1. **ErrorType enum** (`error_type.go`): Generic validation error categories\n2. **String-based error types** (`error_categories.go`): HTTP/API-specific error types\n\nThis analysis documents both systems, their relationships, and integration points for error formatting helpers.\n\n---\n\n## 1. ErrorType Enum (error_type.go)\n\n### Type Definition\n\n```go\ntype ErrorType string\n```\n\n### Enum Values\n\n| Constant | String Value | Purpose | Severity |\n|----------|-------------|---------|----------|\n| `ErrTypeRequired` | `\"required\"` | Required field is missing or empty | High |\n| `ErrTypeFormat` | `\"format\"` | Value format is invalid (email, UUID pattern) | Medium |\n| `ErrTypeRange` | `\"range\"` | Value outside acceptable numeric range | Medium |\n| `ErrTypeLength` | `\"length\"` | String length or collection size invalid | Medium |\n| `ErrTypeType` | `\"type\"` | Value type is incorrect (string vs int) | High |\n| `ErrTypeValue` | `\"value\"` | Value invalid for domain-specific reasons | Low |\n| `ErrTypeDuplicate` | `\"duplicate\"` | Duplicate value detected | High |\n| `ErrTypeConflict` | `\"conflict\"` | Conflict with existing values/constraints | Medium |\n| `ErrTypeUnknown` | `\"unknown\"` | Unknown error type (fallback) | Low |\n\n### Key Methods\n\n- `String() string` - Returns string representation\n- `IsValid() bool` - Validates against known constants\n- `Description() string` - Returns human-readable description\n- `IsRequired()`, `IsFormat()`, etc. - Type checking methods\n\n### ErrorType Collections\n\n```go\n// Grouped by logical category\nStructuralErrorTypes = {ErrTypeRequired, ErrTypeType, ErrTypeLength}\nSemanticErrorTypes = {ErrTypeFormat, ErrTypeRange, ErrTypeValue}\nConstraintErrorTypes = {ErrTypeDuplicate, ErrTypeConflict}\nAllErrorTypes = {all 9 types}\n```\n\n### Constructor Functions\n\n```go\nfunc ErrorTypeFromString(s string) ErrorType  // Returns ErrTypeUnknown if invalid\nfunc MustParseErrorType(s string) ErrorType   // Panics if invalid\n```\n\n---\n\n## 2. String-Based Error Types (error_categories.go)\n\n### Type Constants\n\nThese are string constants for HTTP/API validation scenarios:\n\n```go\nconst (\n    // HTTP status codes\n    ErrorTypeStatusCode          = \"status_code\"\n    ErrorTypeStatusCodeRange     = \"status_code_range\"\n    ErrorTypeStatusCodeClass     = \"status_code_class\"\n\n    // Response content\n    ErrorTypeContentType         = \"content_type\"\n    ErrorTypeResponseStructure   = \"response_structure\"\n    ErrorTypeResponseBody        = \"response_body\"\n    ErrorTypeResponseEncoding    = \"response_encoding\"\n\n    // Error messages\n    ErrorTypeErrorMessage        = \"error_message\"\n    ErrorTypeErrorMessagePattern = \"error_message_pattern\"\n    ErrorTypeErrorCode           = \"error_code\"\n    ErrorTypeErrorDetail         = \"error_detail\"\n\n    // Headers\n    ErrorTypeCORSHeaders         = \"cors_headers\"\n    ErrorTypeAuthHeaders         = \"auth_headers\"\n    ErrorTypeCustomHeaders       = \"custom_headers\"\n\n    // Schema and data\n    ErrorTypeJSONSchema          = \"json_schema\"\n    ErrorTypeDataValidation      = \"data_validation\"\n    ErrorTypeFieldValidation     = \"field_validation\"\n    ErrorTypeTypeValidation      = \"type_validation\"\n\n    // Performance\n    ErrorTypeTimeout             = \"timeout\"\n    ErrorTypeRateLimit           = \"rate_limit\"\n    ErrorTypeRetryExceeded       = \"retry_exceeded\"\n\n    // Custom\n    ErrorTypeCustom              = \"custom\"\n    ErrorTypeUnknown             = \"unknown\"\n)\n```\n\n### Validation Functions\n\n```go\nfunc IsValidErrorType(errorType string) bool\nfunc ValidateErrorType(errorType string) error\n```\n\n---\n\n## 3. ErrorCategory System (error_categories.go)\n\n### Type Definition\n\n```go\ntype ErrorCategory string\n```\n\n### Category Values\n\n| Constant | String Value | Purpose |\n|----------|-------------|---------|\n| `CategoryHTTP` | `\"http\"` | HTTP protocol-level errors (status codes, headers) |\n| `CategoryContent` | `\"content\"` | Response content errors (body, structure, encoding) |\n| `CategoryValidation` | `\"validation\"` | Data validation errors (schema, fields, types) |\n| `CategoryPerformance` | `\"performance\"` | Timing and rate-related errors |\n| `CategorySecurity` | `\"security\"` | Authentication and authorization errors |\n| `CategoryCustom` | `\"custom\"` | Custom application-specific errors |\n\n### String-Based Type to Category Mapping\n\n```go\nvar errorTypeCategoryMap = map[string]ErrorCategory{\n    ErrorTypeStatusCode:          CategoryHTTP,\n    ErrorTypeStatusCodeRange:     CategoryHTTP,\n    ErrorTypeStatusCodeClass:     CategoryHTTP,\n    ErrorTypeContentType:         CategoryHTTP,\n    // ... (all string-based types mapped)\n}\n```\n\n### ErrorType Enum to Category Mapping\n\n```go\nvar categoryForErrorTypeEnum = map[ErrorType]ErrorCategory{\n    ErrTypeRequired:   CategoryValidation,\n    ErrTypeType:       CategoryValidation,\n    ErrTypeLength:     CategoryValidation,\n    ErrTypeFormat:     CategoryValidation,\n    ErrTypeRange:      CategoryValidation,\n    ErrTypeValue:      CategoryValidation,\n    ErrTypeDuplicate:  CategoryValidation,\n    ErrTypeConflict:   CategoryValidation,\n    ErrTypeUnknown:    CategoryCustom,\n}\n```\n\n---\n\n## 4. ErrorSeverity System (error_categorization.go)\n\n### Type Definition\n\n```go\ntype ErrorSeverity string\n```\n\n### Severity Values\n\n| Constant | String Value | Level | Usage |\n|----------|-------------|-------|-------|\n| `SeverityCritical` | `\"critical\"` | 4 | System-wide failures (auth failures) |\n| `SeverityHigh` | `\"high\"` | 3 | Significant impact (missing required field) |\n| `SeverityMedium` | `\"medium\"` | 2 | Partial impact (format issues) |\n| `SeverityLow` | `\"low\"` | 1 | Minimal impact (detail issues) |\n| `SeverityInfo` | `\"info\"` | 0 | Informational only |\n\n### String-Based Type to Severity Mapping\n\n```go\nvar defaultSeverityForErrorType = map[string]ErrorSeverity{\n    ErrorTypeStatusCode:          SeverityHigh,\n    ErrorTypeAuthHeaders:         SeverityCritical,\n    ErrorTypeResponseStructure:   SeverityHigh,\n    // ... (all string-based types have severity)\n}\n```\n\n### ErrorType Enum to Severity Mapping\n\n```go\nvar defaultSeverityForErrorTypeEnum = map[ErrorType]ErrorSeverity{\n    ErrTypeRequired:   SeverityHigh,\n    ErrTypeType:       SeverityHigh,\n    ErrTypeDuplicate:  SeverityHigh,\n    ErrTypeFormat:     SeverityMedium,\n    ErrTypeRange:      SeverityMedium,\n    ErrTypeLength:     SeverityMedium,\n    ErrTypeConflict:   SeverityMedium,\n    ErrTypeValue:      SeverityLow,\n    ErrTypeUnknown:    SeverityLow,\n}\n```\n\n---\n\n## 5. ValidationError Struct (error_types.go)\n\n### Structure\n\n```go\ntype ValidationError struct {\n    // Required fields\n    ErrorType string                   // What kind of validation failed\n    Message   string                   // Human-readable description\n\n    // Optional fields\n    Context           string          // Where/when validation occurred\n    Expected          interface{}     // Expected value\n    Actual            interface{}     // Actual value\n    FieldName         string          // Field where error found\n    Location          string          // Position information\n    RelatedFields     []string        // Related field names\n    PatternDetails    string          // Pattern matching info\n    RangeInfo         string          // Range boundaries\n    ValidationDetails []string        // Additional details\n    ResponseSnippet   string          // Truncated response excerpt\n    Suggestions       []string        // Actionable recommendations\n}\n```\n\n### Key Points\n\n- `ErrorType` is a **string** field that can hold:\n  - ErrorType enum values (e.g., `\"required\"`, `\"format\"`)\n  - String-based error types (e.g., `\"status_code\"`, `\"content_type\"`)\n  - Custom values\n\n- This single field unifies both error type systems\n\n---\n\n## 6. Integration Points with Error Formatting\n\n### Current Error Formatting Functions (error_formatting.go)\n\n#### Field Reference Formatting\n\n```go\nfunc FormatFieldRef(fieldName, parent string) string\nfunc FormatFieldLocationWith(fieldName, location, parent string) string\nfunc FormatFieldListWith(fields []string, conjunction string) string\n```\n\n#### Error Message Formatting\n\n```go\nfunc FormatErrorMessage(errorType, message, fieldName string) string\nfunc FormatErrorMessageWithType(errorType ErrorType, message, fieldName string) string\nfunc FormatErrorWithSeverity(errorType ErrorType, message, fieldName string) string\n```\n\n#### ErrorType Formatting\n\n```go\nfunc FormatErrorType(errorType ErrorType) string\nfunc FormatErrorTypeFrom(errorTypeStr string) string\n```\n\n#### Category-Aware Formatting\n\n```go\nfunc FormatCategory(category ErrorCategory) string\nfunc FormatErrorByCategory(errorType ErrorType, message, fieldName string) string\nfunc FormatErrorWithCategoryAndSeverity(errorType ErrorType, message, fieldName string) string\n```\n\n#### ValidationError Formatting\n\n```go\nfunc FormatValidationErrorFull(err ValidationError, includeSeverity bool) string\nfunc FormatValidationErrorBrief(err ValidationError) string\nfunc FormatValidationErrorWithTypeOverride(err ValidationError, errorType ErrorType, includeSeverity bool) string\nfunc FormatValidationErrorWithAutoType(err ValidationError, includeSeverity bool) string\n```\n\n#### Type Detection Functions\n\n```go\nfunc FormatErrorWithType(errorType ErrorType, message, fieldName string) string\nfunc FormatErrorWithTypeDetection(errorTypeStr string, message, fieldName string) string\n```\n\n### Cross-System Functions (error_categorization.go)\n\n```go\n// Converts between the two systems\nfunc GetCategoryForErrorType(errorType string) ErrorCategory\nfunc GetDefaultSeverityForErrorType(errorType string) ErrorSeverity\n\n// ErrorType enum specific\nfunc GetCategoryForErrorTypeEnum(errorType ErrorType) ErrorCategory\nfunc GetSeverityForErrorTypeEnum(errorType ErrorType) ErrorSeverity\n```\n\n---\n\n## 7. Usage Patterns\n\n### Pattern 1: Using ErrorType Enum (Type-Safe)\n\n```go\n// Create error with enum\nerr := ValidationError{\n    ErrorType: string(ErrTypeRequired),\n    Message:   \"Field is required\",\n    FieldName: \"email\",\n}\n\n// Format with type-safe function\nmsg := FormatErrorWithType(ErrTypeRequired, \"Field is required\", \"email\")\n// Output: \"[required] email: Field is required\"\n\n// Format with automatic severity and category\nmsg := FormatErrorWithCategoryAndSeverity(ErrTypeRequired, \"Field is required\", \"email\")\n// Output: \"[⚠] High [Data Validation] [required] email: Field is required\"\n```\n\n### Pattern 2: Using String-Based Error Types (HTTP/API)\n\n```go\n// Create error with string type\nerr := ValidationError{\n    ErrorType: ErrorTypeStatusCode,\n    Message:   \"Expected status code 200 but got 404\",\n    Expected:  200,\n    Actual:    404,\n}\n\n// Format with string-based function\nmsg := FormatErrorMessage(ErrorTypeStatusCode, \"Status code mismatch\", \"\")\n// Output: \"[status_code] Status code mismatch\"\n\n// Get category and severity\ncategory := GetCategoryForErrorType(ErrorTypeStatusCode)  // CategoryHTTP\nseverity := GetDefaultSeverityForErrorType(ErrorTypeStatusCode)  // SeverityHigh\n```\n\n### Pattern 3: Type Detection (Automatic Conversion)\n\n```go\n// ValidationError with string error type\nerr := ValidationError{\n    ErrorType: \"required\",  // String, but matches ErrorType enum\n    Message:   \"Field is required\",\n    FieldName: \"email\",\n}\n\n// Auto-detect and format with full context\nmsg := FormatErrorWithTypeDetection(\"required\", \"Field is required\", \"email\")\n// Output: \"[⚠] High [required] email: Field is required\"\n\n// Or use ValidationError with auto type detection\nmsg := FormatValidationErrorWithAutoType(err, true)\n// Output: \"[⚠] High [required] email: Field is required\"\n```\n\n### Pattern 4: Full ValidationError Formatting\n\n```go\nerr := ValidationError{\n    ErrorType: string(ErrTypeFormat),\n    Message:   \"Invalid email format\",\n    FieldName: \"email\",\n    Expected:  \"user@domain.com\",\n    Actual:    \"invalid\",\n    Location:  \"line 5\",\n}\n\n// Full format with severity\nmsg := FormatValidationErrorFull(err, true)\n// Output: \"[■] Medium [format] email: Invalid email format (expected: user@domain.com, actual: invalid) [line 5]\"\n\n// Brief format\nmsg := FormatValidationErrorBrief(err)\n// Output: \"email: format\"\n```\n\n---\n\n## 8. Integration Points Summary\n\n### ErrorType Enum Integration\n\n| Function | Uses ErrorType Enum | Purpose |\n|----------|-------------------|---------|\n| `FormatErrorType()` | ✓ | Convert enum to `FormatErrorListSummary()` | ✓ | Summarize by type |\n\n---\n\n## 9. How ErrorType Should Be Used in FormatError\n\n### Recommendation: Unified ErrorType Handling\n\nFormatError functions should support **both ErrorType enum values and string-based error types** through:\n\n1. **Type Detection**: Automatically detect if a string matches an ErrorType enum\n2. **Dual Mapping**: Look up category and severity from either system\n3. **Fallback Handling**: Gracefully handle unknown/custom error types\n\n### Implementation Pattern\n\n```go\n// The recommended pattern for FormatError functions:\nfunc FormatError(err ValidationError) string {\n    // 1. Try to parse ErrorType enum from string\n    errorType := ErrorTypeFromString(err.ErrorType)\n    \n    // 2. Get category and severity (works for both systems)\n    var category ErrorCategory\n    var severity ErrorSeverity\n    \n    if errorType.IsValid() && errorType != ErrTypeUnknown {\n        // Use ErrorType enum mappings\n        category = GetCategoryForErrorTypeEnum(errorType)\n        severity = GetSeverityForErrorTypeEnum(errorType)\n    } else {\n        // Use string-based error type mappings\n        category = GetCategoryForErrorType(err.ErrorType)\n        severity = GetDefaultSeverityForErrorType(err.ErrorType)\n    }\n    \n    // 3. Format with full context\n    return fmt.Sprintf(\"[%s] [%s] %s: %s\",\n        FormatSeverityWithIndicator(severity),\n        FormatCategory(category),\n        err.ErrorType,\n        err.Message)\n}\n```\n\n### Compatibility Requirements\n\n1. **Backward Compatibility**: Must work with existing string-based error types\n2. **Type Safety**: Should prefer ErrorType enum when available\n3. **Graceful Degradation**: Handle unknown error types without panics\n4. **Consistent Output**: Format both systems consistently\n\n---\n\n## 10. Existing Compatibility\n\nThe existing code **already handles both systems correctly**:\n\n### In error_categorization.go\n\n```go\nfunc GetCategoryForErrorType(errorType string) ErrorCategory {\n    // First check string-based map\n    if category, ok := errorTypeCategoryMap[errorType]; ok {\n        return category\n    }\n    \n    // Check if this is an ErrorType enum value\n    et := ErrorTypeFromString(errorType)\n    if et.IsValid() && et != ErrTypeUnknown {\n        return GetCategoryForErrorTypeEnum(et)\n    }\n    \n    return CategoryCustom\n}\n\nfunc GetDefaultSeverityForErrorType(errorType string) ErrorSeverity {\n    // First check string-based map\n    if severity, ok := defaultSeverityForErrorType[errorType]; ok {\n        return severity\n    }\n    \n    // Check if this is an ErrorType enum value\n    et := ErrorTypeFromString(errorType)\n    if et.IsValid() && et != ErrTypeUnknown {\n        return GetSeverityForErrorTypeEnum(et)\n    }\n    \n    return SeverityLow\n}\n```\n\n### In error_formatting.go\n\n```go\nfunc FormatValidationErrorFull(err ValidationError, includeSeverity bool) string {\n    // ... \n    if includeSeverity {\n        // Try ErrorType enum first\n        et := ErrorTypeFromString(err.ErrorType)\n        var severity ErrorSeverity\n        if et.IsValid() && et != ErrTypeUnknown {\n            severity = GetSeverityForErrorTypeEnum(et)\n        } else {\n            // Fall back to string-based lookup\n            severity = GetDefaultSeverityForErrorType(err.ErrorType)\n        }\n        builder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"%s \", FormatSeverityWithIndicator(severity)))\n    }\n    // ...\n}\n```\n\n---\n\n## 11.
